meta.title: ИИ-стек как структурная власть — Combined Master Pack
meta.method: ProfGames-style layered organization. Merge of: (a) v0.4 operational pack (evidence/timeline/claim_checks/country_cards/cyber_pack/cognitive_security/critical_decision_use/palantir_case/weekly_memo/gaps), (b) v0.2 factcheck layer (claims with recommended_phrasing_ru/counterarguments/recommendations), (c) new v0.3 research closing the Stanford-tables and chip-license gaps and adding model-layer export-control claims.
meta.layers_note: 'evidence' = atomic signals (operational). 'claims' = factcheck verification tier (with publish-safe wording, EN+RU). 'claim_checks' = thesis-level checks (RU safe wording). Same event may appear at multiple granularities by design.

passes.name: Scale of the race
passes.approach: Verify investment/compute concentration; separate pledge vs committed vs spend vs capex vs private investment

passes.name: Country positions in the stack
passes.approach: Country cards across bottom (energy/chips/fabs), middle (cloud/cyber/patch), top (models/data/governance); test for real exit option

passes.name: Export controls as leverage
passes.approach: Primary-sourced chronology of controls, countermeasures, forced divestitures, revenue-share/rent

passes.name: Automated vuln discovery/exploitation
passes.approach: Separate proven find-and-fix (AIxCC, OSS-CRS) from contested vendor capability claims; flag disputed where no independent verification

passes.name: AI agents of influence in decision-support
passes.approach: Verify poisoning/prompt-injection research and embedded government deployments; assess novelty vs old supply-chain/bias problems

passes.name: Sovereignty gap and learning curves
passes.approach: Test whether open weights transfer chips/cloud/iteration/tempo or only change the metropole

evidence_levels.A: Primary document / audited data / official filing / official registry / court filing
evidence_levels.B: Reputable wire or major outlet with named sources (Reuters, Bloomberg, FT, WSJ, CNBC, Nikkei, SCMP)
evidence_levels.C: Think tank / academic working paper / preprint / industry analysis (CNAS, CSET, RAND, New America, Chatham House, GovAI, Stanford HAI)
evidence_levels.D: Vendor claim / political speech / sponsored report
evidence_levels.E: Social media / unsourced / rumor (do not use as fact)

status_values.verified: Primary source confirms claim as stated
status_values.partially_verified: Core claim confirmed, some details need adjustment
status_values.needs_correction: Verified but stated incorrectly in original materials
status_values.not_verified: Primary source not located, secondary only
status_values.disputed: One side publicly contests the causal/capability claim

money_status_values.capex_plan: Company capital-expenditure guidance, not realised spend
money_status_values.private_investment: Private/VC investment as tracked by databases
money_status_values.corporate_financial_charge: Accounting charge taken by a company
money_status_values.investment_reported: Investment reported by a secondary source
money_status_values.contract_ceiling: Maximum contract value, not guaranteed spend

meta.source_quality_note: Amounts are not normalized unless money_status is explicitly set. Do not mix pledge, capex plan, allocated budget, committed capital, spent, and deployed capacity.

changelog.description: Merged targeted heatmap backfill from v0.11 research candidates. Added 22 evidence signals, 2 story arcs (data licensing as input layer; cloud capacity/vendor lock-in), and explicit story edges so sparse 2024-2025 model/data/cloud/cyber cells are represented without changing v0.10.
Fixed dangling story arcs in visualization. Added 29 explicit story_arc→thesis edges. hanging_arcs_after_fix=[]. Restored full v0.8 narrative graph and merged v0.9 timeline backfill arcs.

Backfilled 2022-2023 timeline with formation-phase events (+15 timeline rows, +15 evidence rows, +3 claims). Theme: 2022 = industrial policy + first controls + public deployment shock; 2023 = cloud/model lock-in, governance shock, China counter-controls, outbound capital controls, AIxCC launch, and A800/H800 workaround closure.

FINAL pass. Closed GAP_020 (transshipment scale via three DOJ cases: Super Micro ~$2.5B, ALX via Singapore/Malaysia, Janford 400 A100; Taiwan first prosecution) and GAP_021 (data-center cancellations: Sightline/Bloomberg ~5 of 16 GW building, 30-50% delayed/cancelled; Data Center Watch 75+/$130B). Added export-18 (transshipment leakage + enforcement escalation) and scale-12 (energy constraint materially binding the buildout). Cross-linked ca-05/ca-08 to DOJ evidence. Theme reinforced: controls leak yet impose growing cost/delay/risk/policing; energy is a binding-but-contingent bottom-of-stack layer.

Merged the confirm/refute delta: +11 evidence, +5 claim_checks, +5 gaps (GAP_020-024), +3 counterarguments (ca-08/09/10). Added 7 granular factcheck claims with RU phrasing: control leakage/enforcement (export-17, Malaysia seizure), energy-as-stack-layer with flexibility (scale-10), data-center political backlash (scale-11), war data flywheel (cogsec-10), drone mass-militarization + public legitimacy (cogsec-11), finance kill-switch governance (cogsec-12), decision-sovereignty risk+mitigation (cogsec-13). Marked GAP_015 partially addressed; cross-linked ca-05 to the Malaysia seizure. Theme: control need not be airtight to be structural; energy is real but local/software-manageable; war is a data flywheel; finance moved to governed shutdown.

Closed GAP_009 (model-export order primary basis: BIS 'is-informed' letter, Lutnick->Amodei, ECRA 50 USC 4817(b)(1) + EAR 744.22(b); Bloomberg copy; legal validity contested) and GAP_017 (Palantir Army EA primary record: contract W519TC-25-D-0039, $10B ceiling, $0 obligated at award, 75 contracts consolidated). GAP_013 partially addressed. Added export-16 (legal instrument + contested basis) and cogsec-09 (Palantir decision-OS primary). Money-discipline correction: $10B is a ceiling, not spend.

Merged v0.4 operational pack + v0.2 factcheck layer + new v0.3 research. Closed GAP_001 (Stanford AI Index 2026 primary tables: $344.7B global private, $581.7B corporate, $184B-vs-$912B guidance-fund clarification) and GAP_004 (chip toll-and-throttle regime: H200/MI325X case-by-case, Blackwell denial, Huawei 600k Ascend). Added 6 granular claims incl. model-layer export control (export-13/14/15) and the Mythos/360 cyber cluster (cyber-06/07). Imported claims+counterarguments+recommendations that the operational line lacked. Preserved v0.4's Mythos/Fable/360 evidence, critical_decision_use and palantir_case untouched.
(User offline) Expanded operational pack to 61 evidence / 42 timeline / 19 claim_checks / 19 gaps; added critical_decision_use and palantir_case; integrated Mythos/Fable/Glasswing/360 cluster.
Factcheck pack: 50 claims, computed stats, money_status + recommended_phrasing_ru, counterarguments, recommendations.

meta.status_note_final: v0.8 is the consolidated/locked pack as of 2026-06-28. The Mythos/Fable model-export episode and chip-license status are moving targets (litigation/rulemaking ongoing); date-stamp any use. The headline pass-rate is high because contested items are isolated as a small number of disputed flags rather than spread across not_verified; rely on the disputed flags and counterarguments, not the percentage.
meta.update_note: v0.11 heatmap backfill: merged targeted 2023-2025 model, data, cloud/finance and cyber milestones from ai_stack_gap_backfill_candidates_v0_11_research.json.

key_findings: Capital concentration is real and extreme: US private AI investment was $285.9B in 2025 vs China $12.4B (~23x), but this understates China because private-investment databases miss state guidance funds (~$184B into AI 2000-2023). [Stanford AI Index 2026]

The most defensible structural-power fact is the export-control chronology: the US converts private chip sales into leverage (Oct 2022 controls; Jan 2025 AI Diffusion Rule; May 2025 rescission + Huawei GP10 guidance; Aug 2025 15% revenue-share; May 2026 BIS D:5/Macau extraterritorial guidance).

The codified tier order is currently IN FLUX, not settled: the AI Diffusion Rule was rescinded (May 2025) and its draft replacement was withdrawn (March 2026) — phrase the world-tier system as an active project run through discretionary guidance, antitrust (EU DMA on AWS/Azure) and alliance formation (Pax Silica).

Machine-speed offensive cyber at scale is NOT established fact. The proven datapoint is defensive find-and-fix (DARPA AIxCC 86% found / 68% patched; OSS-CRS found 10 real bugs). The headline offensive claim (Anthropic GTG-1002) is contested — no IoCs, admitted hallucinations, no government corroboration.

Cognitive influence moved from lab demo to measured in-the-wild prevalence: 15.3K validated indirect-prompt-injection instances across 1.2B URLs; ~1% of 200K resumes carry hidden injections; 8,648 successful agent injections over 272K attempts. The missing piece is a confirmed government/enterprise decision-support incident.

Data/model poisoning is a credible knowledge-structure attack surface: ~250 documents (0.00016% of data) can backdoor models regardless of size (Anthropic/UK AISI/Alan Turing). AI decision-support is embedded at the security core (DoD $200M-ceiling deals to four labs; Claude via Palantir in classified workflows).

Gulf/UK/India/EU/Russia sovereign AI is better framed as MANAGED DEPENDENCE: access to frontier compute under external assurance and alignment conditions (G42 stripped Huawei gear; Stargate UK paused; IndiaAI and Amazon $13B build on Nvidia/foreign cloud; Russia is isolated autarky bottlenecked by CUDA).

Open weights create an exit at the model-artifact layer (DeepSeek R1/V4, Qwen, Kimi, GLM-5 on Ascend/SMIC) but do not automatically transfer learning curves: DeepSeek reportedly reverted R2 to Nvidia after Ascend instability; SMIC ~30-50% 7nm yields remain binding.

NEW (2026): export controls reached the MODEL layer — on 12 June 2026 Commerce ordered Anthropic to bar all foreign-national access to Fable 5 / Mythos 5 (license now required to export/transfer the models); Anthropic disabled them worldwide, the DoD branded Anthropic a 'supply-chain risk', Anthropic sued, and Mythos 5 was later cleared to ~100 vetted US orgs. A frontier model treated as a controlled munition.

NEW (2026): the chip lever is a graduated toll-and-throttle regime, not a wall — BIS put H200/MI325X on case-by-case review (25% tariff, 50% cap, KYC, US inspection), Blackwell stays denied, H20 flows under the 15% deal, and Beijing throttles imports from its side while Huawei targets 600k Ascend 910C in 2026.

NEW (2026): Stanford AI Index 2026 primary aggregates — global private AI investment $344.7B (+127.5%), corporate $581.7B (+130%); guidance-fund clarification: ~$184B into AI (2000-2023), NOT the $912B all-industry figure many recaps misuse.

NEW (2026): the Mythos cyber 'weapon' is best framed as ACCESS ASYMMETRY (one-way transparency: ~40 Glasswing orgs, US-persons-only), not a settled machine-speed-0day leap — Anthropic itself says the flagged jailbreak found only minor known bugs and comparable capability exists elsewhere; China's 360 'Mythos-equivalent' counter-claim is unverified by Reuters.

NEW (v0.6): the model-export directive's legal instrument is a BIS 'is-informed' letter (Lutnick->Amodei) under ECRA 50 USC 4817(b)(1) + EAR 744.22(b) — first use against an AI model — but its validity is openly contested (services arguably outside export law; worldwide scope exceeds the statute's country list; First Amendment), with 80+ security leaders and G7 governments urging restoration; the government has not published the order.

NEW (v0.6): Palantir primary record — the U.S. Army Enterprise Agreement (W519TC-25-D-0039, 31 Jul 2025) consolidates 75 contracts into one 10-year vehicle with a $10B CEILING but $0 obligated at award. Cite as maximum potential value, not spend; the structural point is consolidation/lock-in into a single decision-and-data vendor.

NEW (v0.7): controls leak but also police — Malaysia seized 72 AI-chip servers (~$12.93M) at KLIA in June 2026 (declared as computer components, bound for re-export). Structural power is cost/delay/risk/surveillance, not perfect denial; leverage need not be airtight to be structural.

NEW (v0.7): energy/grid is a real bottom-of-stack layer (six firms ~118 TWh 2024 -> 239-295 TWh 2030, regional stress) but NOT absolute — GPU clusters can be grid-responsive (curtail/shift load), cutting grid costs 3-21% in some settings; frame energy as local and software-manageable, and note a US public backlash (poll: ~half back a temporary build ban).

NEW (v0.7): war is a data flywheel — Ukraine's military is the largest AI consumer with 500k+ hours of drone footage as training data; South Korea is mass-militarizing the drone/AI layer (500k operators, Chinese-component exclusion); publics are conditionally permissive on military AI but draw the line at fully autonomous lethal force.

NEW (v0.7): finance moved from AI adoption to governed shutdown — RBI's draft framework adds board accountability, independent validation, human oversight and decommissioning ('kill-switch') logic; the precise sovereignty risk is supplier boundary control, mitigable via model replaceability and state-owned orchestration.

NEW (v0.8): transshipment leakage is documented and large (DOJ Super Micro ~$2.5B / $510M in weeks; ALX via Singapore/Malaysia; Janford 400 A100 via Malaysia/Thailand) AND enforcement is escalating (DOJ's largest-ever AI-hardware case, Taiwan's first criminal prosecution, Malaysia permits/seizure). Controls leak yet impose growing cost/delay/risk/policing; total gray-market volume remains unquantified.

NEW (v0.8): the energy bottleneck is materially binding the US buildout (only ~5 of ~16 GW of 2026 capacity under construction; 30-50% delayed/cancelled; transformers 3-5yr, switchgear through 2028, interconnection 3-7yr; 75+ projects / ~$130B blocked in Q1 2026) — real but, per the flexibility evidence, regionally and technologically contingent.

NEW (v0.11): heatmap backfill shows 2024-2025 model/data/cloud/cyber cells were under-collected, not empty: GPT-4o, Claude 3/3.5, Gemini 1.5, Llama 3.1/4, DeepSeek-R1, Oracle/OpenAI capacity, Amazon/Anthropic, Reddit/Google data API, News Corp/OpenAI, Big Sleep and OpenAI/Microsoft threat-actor disruption add missing model-access, data-licensing, cloud-capacity and defensive-cyber milestones.

evidence.title: BIS launches advanced-computing and semiconductor-manufacturing controls on China
evidence.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Industry and Security
evidence.claim_supported: Export controls convert AI compute and semiconductor supply chains into geopolitical chokepoints.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add Federal Register text and exact ECCN changes in final legal appendix.
evidence.notes: Baseline event for the export-control chronology.

evidence.title: U.S. expands chip restrictions to HBM, manufacturing equipment/software, and 140 China-linked entities
evidence.claim_supported: The control regime widens from GPUs to the wider semiconductor production stack.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Attach primary BIS rule/Federal Register notice for final publication.
evidence.notes: Use as control-stack broadening: HBM, equipment, software, FDPR and Entity List.

evidence.title: AI Diffusion Rule creates explicit country tiers for AI chip access
evidence.claim_supported: The AI stack creates formalized geopolitical tiers of access.
evidence.claim_challenged: The exact tier architecture is unstable and later partially reversed.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Final legal summary must incorporate May 2025 rescission/non-enforcement and March 2026 draft withdrawal.
evidence.notes: Use as evidence for tiering, not as a settled permanent regime.

evidence.title: Commerce withdraws planned replacement rule for AI chip exports
evidence.claim_supported: Export-control architecture is dynamic and contested.
evidence.claim_challenged: A static three-tier world system cannot be stated without qualification.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Track any successor rule after March 2026.
evidence.notes: Important reviewer-proofing signal.

evidence.title: BIS clarifies D:5/Macau-headquartered entities abroad still need licenses for advanced computing items
evidence.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Industry and Security
evidence.claim_supported: Control follows headquarters/ultimate parentage, not only physical destination.
evidence.exact_quote_short: license is required to export advanced computing items to entities headquartered in Country Group D:5 or Macau
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add examples of enforcement or denied licenses if available.
evidence.notes: Strong evidence for extraterritorial or ownership-based control logic.

evidence.title: Nvidia expects up to $5.5B charge after U.S. licensing requirement on H20 sales to China
evidence.claim_supported: State export controls turn private chip sales into geopolitical and financial leverage.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add Nvidia 10-Q/8-K if used as slide number.
evidence.notes: Clean slide fact for weaponized interdependence.

evidence.title: Microsoft-G42 deal viewed positively by White House because G42 cut Huawei ties
evidence.claim_supported: Access to frontier AI/cloud can be conditioned on geopolitical alignment and supply-chain assurance.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add Microsoft/G42 contract terms if public.
evidence.notes: Core case for Gulf protectorate / conditional access.

evidence.title: Stargate UAE AI datacenter to begin operation in 2026, with broader 5GW plan
evidence.actor: G42, OpenAI, Oracle, Nvidia, Cisco, SoftBank, U.S. Commerce Department
evidence.claim_supported: Gulf sovereign AI buildout depends on U.S.-anchored frontier stack and compliance oversight.
evidence.claim_challenged: This is not full-stack sovereignty if compute, chips and models remain externally governed.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Track actual GPU delivery, operational MW and compliance working-group outputs.
evidence.notes: Use 'planned capacity' language.

evidence.title: Saudi HUMAIN invested $3B in xAI Series E; xAI/HUMAIN plan 500MW AI data-center infrastructure
evidence.claim_supported: Gulf states are buying influence and access into frontier AI infrastructure.
evidence.claim_challenged: Equity participation and datacenter plans do not equal full-stack sovereignty.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add official HUMAIN/xAI announcement if available.
evidence.notes: Country-card evidence for Gulf sovereign buyer tier.

evidence.title: EU InvestAI / AI gigafactory push seeks to narrow frontier-compute gap
evidence.claim_supported: Europe is using public-private industrial policy to build compute capacity.
evidence.claim_challenged: Bids and policy commitments are not yet operating frontier capacity.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add European Commission primary pages, EuroHPC allocations, and deployment status.
evidence.notes: Use for EU semi-periphery with public compute.

evidence.title: EU preliminarily concludes AWS and Azure should fall under DMA gatekeeper rules
evidence.claim_supported: Cloud concentration is an AI-stack dependency and governance problem.
evidence.claim_challenged: Final regulatory status and remedies are not yet settled.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Track final DMA designation and remedy details.
evidence.notes: Strong EU semi-periphery / cloud sovereignty signal.

evidence.title: EU joins U.S.-led Pax Silica initiative on AI and chip supply-chain security
evidence.claim_supported: Trusted AI/chip supply-chain blocs are forming around the stack.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add declaration text and member list.
evidence.notes: Useful for bloc formation / supply-chain alignment.

evidence.title: Alibaba plans 380B yuan investment in AI and cloud infrastructure over three years
evidence.claim_supported: China's counter-stack continues to scale capital expenditure despite controls.
evidence.claim_challenged: Announced investment is not the same as deployed frontier capacity.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add Alibaba filing/earnings-call transcript.
evidence.notes: Use as counter-core evidence.

evidence.title: China's push for green power use in AI data centers faces grid and load hurdles
evidence.claim_supported: AI-stack power starts below chips: energy and grid constraints become strategic bottlenecks.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add official Chinese energy/data-center policy documents.
evidence.notes: Good fact for 'compute is energy policy'.

evidence.title: Amazon announces additional $13B investment in India AI/cloud infrastructure by 2030
evidence.claim_supported: India's AI infrastructure growth is materially mediated by foreign hyperscalers.
evidence.claim_challenged: Foreign cloud investment is not the same as domestic sovereign stack control.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add IndiaAI Mission official compute numbers and domestic procurement data.
evidence.notes: Country-card evidence for managed dependence.

evidence.title: Blackstone plans $30B investment in Japan AI data centers, reportedly over 1GW
evidence.claim_supported: AI infrastructure buildout is spreading through global data-center capital markets.
evidence.claim_challenged: Private DC investment does not by itself prove sovereign control.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Need Blackstone primary confirmation and site details.
evidence.notes: Good for finance-rent layer.

evidence.title: Russia proposes sweeping powers to ban or restrict foreign AI tools
evidence.actor: Russian Ministry for Digital Development / Russian government
evidence.claim_supported: Russia is moving toward isolated/autarkic AI governance and data localization.
evidence.claim_challenged: Localization and bans are not evidence of frontier-stack parity.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add Russian draft law text and status.
evidence.notes: Strong country-card item, weak on actual compute capacity.

evidence.title: Post-Yandex Nebius plans over $1B AI infrastructure investment in Europe
evidence.claim_supported: Some Russian-origin AI infrastructure exits Russia and reconnects to Western GPU/cloud stack after corporate divorce.
evidence.claim_challenged: Nebius should not be coded as Russian sovereign capability after split.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add corporate filings and sanctions-status details.
evidence.notes: Important caution for Russia case study.

evidence.title: AIxCC finalists found 77% of injected bugs, patched 61%, and found 18 real-world vulnerabilities
evidence.claim_supported: Automated vulnerability discovery and patching is advancing materially.
evidence.claim_challenged: Does not prove autonomous offensive exploitation at strategic scale.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Add DARPA primary post and competition technical report if published.
evidence.notes: Cyber pack: verified competition/benchmark evidence.

evidence.title: OSS-CRS ports AIxCC CRS techniques to real-world open-source projects
evidence.claim_supported: AIxCC-style cyber reasoning can transfer from competition systems to real OSS targets.
evidence.claim_challenged: Preprint and tool demonstration still need independent adoption evidence.
evidence.exact_quote_short: discovered 10 previously unknown bugs
evidence.corroboration_needed: Peer review, independent replication, and CVE/patch trail.
evidence.notes: Cyber pack: peer/preprint research, not operational attack evidence.

evidence.title: Large-scale study finds indirect prompt injections in the wild across 1.2B URLs
evidence.claim_supported: External web data can become a machine-facing influence channel for AI agents/RAG systems.
evidence.claim_challenged: Mechanism/prevalence evidence is stronger than evidence of strategic state exploitation.
evidence.exact_quote_short: 15.3K validated instances across 11.7K pages
evidence.corroboration_needed: Enterprise/government incident examples.
evidence.notes: Cognitive security pack: strong mechanism evidence.

evidence.title: Real-world resume-screening study finds hidden prompt injections in about 1% of resumes
evidence.claim_supported: Prompt injection has moved from conceptual risk into observed AI-mediated evaluation workflows.
evidence.claim_challenged: Resume-screening evidence should not be generalized to all government or enterprise decision-support without additional cases.
evidence.exact_quote_short: approximately 1% of resumes contain hidden prompt injections
evidence.corroboration_needed: Hiring-platform incident reports and mitigations.
evidence.notes: Good example of knowledge-structure capture.

evidence.title: Public red-team competition finds frontier AI agents vulnerable to indirect prompt injection
evidence.claim_supported: Agentic systems remain structurally vulnerable to hidden-instruction attacks.
evidence.claim_challenged: Competition environment is not a census of production systems.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Production incident evidence.
evidence.notes: Use as mechanism/benchmark evidence.

evidence.title: AI Agent Index documents 30 deployed agentic AI systems and uneven transparency
evidence.claim_supported: Agentic AI systems are deployed faster than documentation and safety transparency mature.
evidence.exact_quote_short: documents information ... of 30 state-of-the-art AI agents
evidence.corroboration_needed: Update with live index data before final publication.
evidence.notes: Background for decision-support/cognition layer.

evidence.title: Anthropic Mythos / Project Glasswing becomes public as restricted cyber-vulnerability model program
evidence.source_name: Washington Post / Wired / Reuters follow-on reporting
evidence.claim_supported: A frontier model can be treated as a restricted cybersecurity capability rather than a general software product.
evidence.claim_challenged: Public details are mediated through press and partner reports; Anthropic primary materials were not located in this pass.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Locate official Anthropic Project Glasswing statement, participant list and access terms.
evidence.notes: Use as model-layer chokepoint signal, not as proof of all Mythos capability claims.

evidence.title: Mozilla reports Mythos-assisted discovery/fixing of 271 Firefox security vulnerabilities
evidence.claim_supported: AI-assisted vulnerability discovery can materially increase defender-side remediation throughput in real software.
evidence.claim_challenged: Partner-reported result; not a public reproducible benchmark and not proof of autonomous offense.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Locate Mozilla primary blog/advisory and map bugs to CVE/security advisories.
evidence.notes: Defender evidence. Do not include exploit details.

evidence.title: AP/Reuters: Mythos found vulnerabilities in classified U.S. government systems during controlled testing
evidence.claim_supported: Government concern about cyber-capable frontier models is tied to tested performance against sensitive systems.
evidence.claim_challenged: Reporting distinguishes vulnerability identification from exploit execution; system details remain classified.
evidence.corroboration_needed: Congressional hearing transcript, official NSA/Cyber Command statement, or declassified test methodology.
evidence.notes: Strong narrative fact, weak technical granularity.

evidence.title: U.S. order reportedly forces Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access globally
evidence.claim_supported: The model artifact/service layer itself is becoming an object of export control, not just chips and cloud.
evidence.claim_challenged: Underlying government order not located as public primary document; details may change.
evidence.exact_quote_short: disabled its most advanced AI models - Mythos 5 and Fable 5 - for all users after the government's June 12 export control order
evidence.corroboration_needed: Public order, license text, or Commerce Department notice.
evidence.notes: This is the key new signal for 'AI-stack as structural power' at the model layer.

evidence.title: U.S. allows limited redeployment of Claude Mythos 5 to trusted U.S. critical-infrastructure organizations
evidence.actor: U.S. government, Anthropic, critical infrastructure organizations
evidence.claim_supported: Access to frontier cyber models is tiered by trust status and critical-infrastructure role.
evidence.claim_challenged: Exact approved organization list and license terms not public.
evidence.exact_quote_short: redeploy ... to over 100 'trusted' U.S. organizations
evidence.corroboration_needed: Official license terms and list criteria.
evidence.notes: Turns 'trusted users' into a governance category.

evidence.title: Reuters/Axios: U.S. close to allowing Anthropic to restore Fable 5 access
evidence.claim_supported: Model-level export controls are being negotiated dynamically rather than operating as a stable rulebook.
evidence.claim_challenged: Reuters could not independently confirm Axios' report at publication time.
evidence.exact_quote_short: Reuters could not immediately confirm the report
evidence.corroboration_needed: Anthropic/Commerce update confirming restoration.
evidence.notes: Use in timeline as weak/soft signal, not settled fact.

evidence.title: OpenAI defers public rollout of GPT-5.6 and begins limited preview at U.S. government request
evidence.claim_supported: Government pre-release access and vetted rollout are becoming a model-governance mechanism for frontier AI.
evidence.claim_challenged: OpenAI describes the limitation as temporary and undesirable as a long-term default.
evidence.exact_quote_short: limiting the AI model's initial access to a small group of vetted partners
evidence.corroboration_needed: OpenAI primary blog post and EO text.
evidence.notes: Generalizes beyond Anthropic: model governance regime is broader than Mythos.

evidence.title: Qihoo 360 unveils Yitian Tulong: Tulongfeng vulnerability agent and Yitianzhen automated defense system
evidence.source_name: Reuters; Habr transcript of Zhou Hongyi speech
evidence.claim_supported: China's counter-stack response may route around weaker base models/chips via agent harness, security data and workflow integration.
evidence.claim_challenged: Capability claims are vendor assertions; Reuters could not independently verify vulnerability counts.
evidence.exact_quote_short: Reuters could not independently verify the claims
evidence.corroboration_needed: Chinese regulator confirmations, vulnerability IDs, Microsoft/other acknowledgements, independent benchmark.
evidence.notes: Do not code as verified Mythos-equivalent capability.

evidence.title: Zhou Hongyi frames vulnerability-finding AI as strategic deterrence and one-way-transparency problem
evidence.source_name: Habr translated transcript of ISC.AI 2026 keynote
evidence.claim_supported: Chinese cyber leadership is explicitly interpreting Mythos-like systems as strategic capability and deterrence, not just product competition.
evidence.claim_challenged: Transcript is reconstructed/translated; many figures are unverified speech claims.
evidence.exact_quote_short: самый важный вопрос: кто обладает способностью непрерывно находить уязвимости?
evidence.corroboration_needed: Chinese original transcript/video, 360 slides, regulator database, partner acknowledgements.
evidence.notes: Valuable as strategic narrative and actor perception even where facts are not verified.

evidence.title: Benchmarking Mythos-linked bug rediscovery finds limited success under favorable target-file scaffolds
evidence.claim_supported: Independent stress tests can challenge broad Mythos capability narratives.
evidence.claim_challenged: Simple prompting or target-file scaffolding does not reproduce a '0-day factory' at high reliability.
evidence.exact_quote_short: These results do not refute Anthropic's undisclosed workflow
evidence.corroboration_needed: Peer review, reproduction on more tasks, compare with actual Mythos workflow if disclosed.
evidence.notes: Must be used as caveat against overclaiming machine-speed 0-day.

evidence.title: Red-team study finds Fable 5 and Opus 4.8 remain breakable under sustained automated jailbreak pressure
evidence.claim_supported: Even hardened frontier models may retain residual automated-jailbreak surfaces.
evidence.claim_challenged: Aggregate safety pass rates should not be interpreted as full containment.
evidence.exact_quote_short: frontier models remain reliably breakable under sustained automated pressure
evidence.corroboration_needed: Independent red-team replication and model-version confirmation.
evidence.notes: Supports government-review logic, but do not conflate jailbreak completions with operational cyber exploitation.

corrections: Label $500B as a pledge, not committed capital
claims.recommended_phrasing: Stargate is a $500B/10GW four-year intention announced Jan 2025; treat it as a pledge, not committed capital — OpenAI claims rapid progress while independent reporting (Bloomberg, The Information) flagged early funding gaps and partner disputes.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Stargate — это четырёхлетнее намерение на $500 млрд / 10 ГВт, объявленное в январе 2025; это pledge, а не committed capital — OpenAI заявляет о быстром прогрессе, тогда как независимые источники (Bloomberg, The Information) фиксировали разрывы в финансировании и споры партнёров на старте.

claims.claim: The US hosts 5,427 data centers, more than ten times any other country.

sources.title: Stanford AI Index 2026 (via TNW)

caveats: Counts vary by definition of data center
claims.recommended_phrasing: Physical infrastructure is concentrated in the US, which hosts 5,427 data centers — more than ten times any other country (Stanford AI Index 2026).

claims.claim: The performance gap between the best US and Chinese models narrowed to ~2.7% by March 2026, from 17.5-31.6 points across MMLU/MATH/HumanEval in 2023.

sources.title: 2026 AI Index Report

caveats: Benchmark leadership trades back and forth; benchmark scores are imperfect proxies
claims.recommended_phrasing: Capital concentration has not bought a durable capability moat: the top US-China model gap narrowed to ~2.7% by early 2026 (Stanford AI Index 2026).

claims.claim: Alibaba announced a 380B yuan (~$52.4B reported) investment in AI and cloud infrastructure over three years (Feb 2025), evidence the Chinese counter-stack keeps scaling capex despite controls.

sources.title: Alibaba to invest more than $52.4B in AI over next 3 years (Reuters)

caveats: Three-year plan, not realised spend
USD figure is the reported conversion
claims.recommended_phrasing: China's counter-stack keeps scaling: Alibaba alone committed 380B yuan (~$52.4B) to AI and cloud over three years (Feb 2025) — a capex plan, not yet realised spend, but a signal that export controls have not frozen Chinese buildout.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Китайский counter-stack продолжает наращивать масштаб: одна Alibaba заявила 380 млрд юаней (~$52.4 млрд) на AI и облако за три года (февраль 2025) — это план capex, ещё не подтверждённый расход, но сигнал, что экспортный контроль не заморозил китайский buildout.

claims.claim: Blackstone plans a reported $30B investment in Japanese AI data centers (>1GW), evidence that AI-infrastructure buildout is spreading through global data-center capital markets beyond the US-China core.

sources.title: Blackstone plans $30B investment in Japan AI data centres (Reuters/Nikkei)

caveats: Nikkei-sourced; needs Blackstone primary confirmation and site details
Planned, not deployed
claims.recommended_phrasing: The buildout is globalising through capital markets, not just hyperscalers: Blackstone reportedly plans $30B for Japanese AI data centers (>1GW, June 2026) — a planned investment via Nikkei, awaiting primary confirmation.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Buildout глобализируется через рынки капитала, а не только гиперскейлеров: Blackstone, по сообщениям, планирует $30 млрд на японские AI-дата-центры (>1 ГВт, июнь 2026) — это план по данным Nikkei, требует первичного подтверждения.

claims.claim: The US holds the full stack: leading chips (Nvidia ~60%+ GPU share), cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP ~66% of cloud infra), frontier closed models, and the chokepoint software layer (CUDA).

sources.title: Stanford 2026 AI Index (via BigGo)
Hyperscaler cloud share

caveats: Market-share figures are estimates
Talent inflow to US fell sharply (Stanford: -89% since 2017) — a counter-trend
claims.recommended_phrasing: The US is the incumbent full-stack core — Nvidia silicon, the big-three clouds (~66% of cloud infra), frontier models and the CUDA software lock — though its talent-attraction advantage is eroding.

claims.claim: China is building a counter-core stack: SMIC 7nm fabrication, Huawei Ascend accelerators (910C/910D), domestic clouds, and competitive open-weight models (DeepSeek, Qwen, Kimi K2, Zhipu GLM-5).

sources.title: How Huawei Trains DeepSeek-R1-Class LLMs
DeepSeek, Huawei, Export Controls (CSIS)

caveats: Ascend remains ~1 generation behind on single-chip performance (Ren Zhengfei); HBM and SMIC yield are bottlenecks
Some performance/training claims are vendor-stated
claims.recommended_phrasing: China is assembling a parallel stack — SMIC 7nm, Huawei Ascend, domestic clouds and a deep open-weight model bench (DeepSeek, Qwen, Kimi, GLM-5) — but stays a generation behind on single-chip performance and constrained by HBM and foundry yields.

claims.claim: Zhipu's GLM-5 (744B MoE) was reportedly trained on ~100,000 Huawei Ascend chips, while DeepSeek reportedly reverted R2 to Nvidia after Ascend training-stability problems.

sources.title: How China's GLM-5 Works
DeepSeek V4 and R2 Deep Dive

caveats: GLM-5 chip count and some self-reported benchmarks not independently verified
DeepSeek R2/Ascend reversion is reported (SiliconAngle), not officially confirmed
corrections: Mark GLM-5 chip count and DeepSeek R2 reversion as reported/unconfirmed
claims.recommended_phrasing: The Ascend training story is mixed: GLM-5 was reportedly trained on ~100,000 Ascend chips, yet DeepSeek reportedly reverted R2 to Nvidia after Ascend instability — evidence that domestic silicon can serve and increasingly train, but not yet frictionlessly at the frontier.

claims.claim: China's push for green-powered AI data centers faces grid and load hurdles: an 80% renewables target by 2030 against ~11% reported share in 2023, with +300-500 TWh of projected additional AI demand by 2030.

sources.title: China green power for AI projects faces hurdles (Reuters)

caveats: Targets and demand projections are forward estimates
Needs official Chinese energy/data-center policy documents
claims.recommended_phrasing: The stack begins below the chips: China's AI buildout collides with grid limits — an 80% renewables target by 2030 against ~11% in 2023 and +300-500 TWh of new AI demand — making energy a strategic bottleneck, not a footnote.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Стек начинается ниже чипов: китайский AI-buildout упирается в ограничения сети — цель 80% ВИЭ к 2030 против ~11% в 2023 и +300-500 ТВт·ч нового спроса от AI — что делает энергетику стратегическим bottleneck, а не сноской.

claims.claim: The UAE's G42 received a $1.5B Microsoft investment (Apr 2024) conditioned on stripping Huawei gear and divesting Chinese holdings; Stargate UAE (1GW, Abu Dhabi) was announced May 2025 with G42/OpenAI/Oracle/Nvidia/Cisco/SoftBank.

sources.title: Microsoft and U.S. join AI forces in UAE deal
Advanced AI chips cleared for export to UAE

caveats: Divested Chinese holdings reportedly moved to a fund under the same ruling-family principal (Bloomberg)
claims.recommended_phrasing: The UAE shows the assurance mechanism cleanly: G42 got Microsoft capital and US chips only after stripping Huawei gear and divesting Chinese stakes — access purchased under external security conditions, the opposite of autonomous sovereignty.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: ОАЭ наглядно показывают механизм assurance: G42 получил капитал Microsoft и американские чипы только после удаления оборудования Huawei и продажи китайских долей — доступ, купленный под внешними условиями безопасности, противоположность автономному суверенитету.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia's PIF-owned HUMAIN agreed (Nov 2025) to deploy up to 600,000 Nvidia AI units over three years (incl. GB300), formed an AMD/Cisco JV targeting up to 1GW by 2030, partnered with xAI on a 500MW Riyadh data center, and invested $3B in xAI's Series E (Feb 2026).

sources.title: HUMAIN Expands Strategic Partnership with NVIDIA
Saudi HUMAIN invested $3B in xAI Series E (Reuters)

caveats: Deployment is multi-year intention dependent on US export licensing
Full-stack branding is vendor language; foundational chips/models are US-supplied
claims.recommended_phrasing: Saudi HUMAIN announced deals to deploy up to 600,000 Nvidia units over three years, an AMD/Cisco 1GW JV, an xAI/Grok deployment and a $3B stake in xAI (Feb 2026) — but the stack rests on US chips, clouds and models under US licensing: bought access, not sovereignty.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Саудовская HUMAIN объявила о развёртывании до 600 000 единиц Nvidia за три года, JV с AMD/Cisco на 1 ГВт, размещении xAI/Grok и доле $3 млрд в xAI (февраль 2026) — но стек держится на американских чипах, облаках и моделях под лицензированием США: купленный доступ, а не суверенитет.

claims.claim: Stargate UK (Sept 2025, OpenAI/Nvidia/Nscale) planned up to 8,000 GPUs scaling to 31,000 for sovereign UK compute; Nscale/Microsoft separately committed ~23,040 GB300 GPUs at Loughton. OpenAI later paused Stargate UK citing energy costs and regulation.

sources.title: Introducing Stargate UK
Stargate UK paused (Data Centre Magazine)

caveats: Sovereign is OpenAI framing; capacity is US-owned models on US chips in UK-sited DCs
Pause shows fragility of the affiliate position
claims.recommended_phrasing: The UK is a platform affiliate: Stargate UK promised sovereign compute (8,000 to 31,000 GPUs) but is OpenAI models on Nvidia chips in UK-sited Nscale DCs — and OpenAI paused it over UK energy costs and regulation, underscoring how conditional that access is.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Великобритания — platform affiliate: Stargate UK обещал суверенный compute (8 000 → 31 000 GPU), но это модели OpenAI на чипах Nvidia в дата-центрах Nscale на территории UK — и OpenAI поставил проект на паузу из-за британских цен на энергию и регулирования, что подчёркивает условность этого доступа.

claims.claim: The EU launched InvestAI (Feb 2025) to mobilise EUR 200B for AI, including a EUR 20B fund for up to five AI gigafactories (>=100,000 advanced chips each), federated with EuroHPC AI Factories.

sources.title: AI Factories — European Commission
EIB-Commission gigafactories MoU

caveats: EUR 200B is a mobilisation target (public+private leverage), not committed spend
~95% of commercially available AI compute is US/China-operated (Hawkins et al. via Interface)
claims.recommended_phrasing: The EU's InvestAI (Feb 2025) aims to mobilise EUR 200B including EUR 20B for AI gigafactories — but it remains a leverage target, and Europe still depends on US/China-operated compute, with ASML its main genuine chokepoint asset.

claims.claim: The EU preliminarily concluded (June 2026) that AWS and Azure should fall under DMA gatekeeper rules, and joined the US-led Pax Silica initiative on AI/chip supply-chain security — signs that cloud concentration is now a governance issue and that trusted-stack blocs are forming.

sources.title: Amazon, Microsoft cloud should fall under EU tech rules (Reuters)
EU joins US-led Pax Silica (Reuters)

caveats: DMA gatekeeper finding is preliminary; track final designation and remedies
Pax Silica declaration text and member list not yet attached
claims.recommended_phrasing: Two June-2026 moves sharpen the picture: the EU preliminarily put AWS and Azure under DMA gatekeeper rules (cloud concentration as a competition/autonomy issue) and joined the US-led Pax Silica supply-chain bloc — the stack is now governed through both antitrust and alliance formation, not just export bans.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Два шага июня 2026 уточняют картину: ЕС предварительно отнёс AWS и Azure к gatekeeper-ам по DMA (облачная концентрация как вопрос конкуренции/автономии) и присоединился к US-led блоку Pax Silica по supply-chain — стек теперь управляется и через антимонополию, и через формирование альянсов, а не только запреты на экспорт.

claims.claim: France hosts Mistral (Europe's main frontier-model contender, backed by Nvidia) and the EU's lithography chokepoint sits in the Netherlands (ASML), the single non-US/China structural-power asset in the stack.

sources.title: Who Controls Europe's AI Future? (Open Future)

caveats: Mistral is Nvidia-backed and integrated into Nvidia's stack — partial dependence
ASML is Dutch, not French; grouped here as EU-bloc leverage
claims.recommended_phrasing: Europe's one true chokepoint is ASML lithography (Netherlands); France's Mistral is the bloc's credible model lab but is Nvidia-backed and ecosystem-dependent — leverage at one layer, dependence at others.

claims.claim: The IndiaAI Mission (Rs 10,371.92 crore / ~$1.25B) assembled ~34,000+ GPUs (target 100,000 by end-2026) at subsidised rates (100% compute subsidy for foundational-model builders), almost entirely on Nvidia/foreign silicon.

sources.title: PIB: India common computing facility
India Fuels Its AI Mission With NVIDIA

caveats: GPU counts grew through 2025-26 (18,693 to ~34,000); indigenous GPU is a 4-year promise
Subsidy rates and totals vary by source
claims.recommended_phrasing: India exemplifies the sovereignty gap: a ~$1.25B IndiaAI Mission subsidising ~34,000+ GPUs (target 100,000) and domestic models (Sarvam, BharatGen) — real capability, but built on Nvidia silicon and foreign cloud, i.e. managed dependence not exit.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Индия — пример sovereignty gap: миссия IndiaAI на ~$1.25 млрд субсидирует ~34 000+ GPU (цель 100 000) и отечественные модели (Sarvam, BharatGen) — реальная компетенция, но построенная на кремнии Nvidia и иностранном облаке, то есть управляемая зависимость, а не exit.

claims.claim: Amazon announced an additional $13B investment in India AI/cloud infrastructure by 2030 ($48B total planned), evidence that India's AI capacity growth is materially mediated by foreign hyperscalers.

sources.title: Amazon to invest additional $13B in India (Reuters)

caveats: Announced plan to 2030, not deployed capacity
Needs IndiaAI Mission official compute and domestic-procurement data alongside
claims.recommended_phrasing: India's dependence is reinforced from the top: Amazon announced an extra $13B for India AI/cloud by 2030 ($48B total planned) — Global South capacity growth routed through a US hyperscaler, exactly the managed-dependence pattern.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Зависимость Индии усиливается сверху: Amazon объявил дополнительные $13 млрд на AI/облако в Индии к 2030 ($48 млрд суммарно) — рост мощностей Global South, маршрутизированный через американского гиперскейлера, ровно паттерн управляемой зависимости.

claims.claim: Russia pursues isolated AI autarky: Sber's GigaChat and Yandex's YandexGPT, blocked from Nvidia by sanctions, with Sberbank exploring Chinese chips and citing CUDA as Nvidia's key lock-in; Russia markets sovereign AI to the Global South and (March 2026) proposed powers to ban/restrict foreign AI tools.

sources.title: Russia's Sberbank offers AI to Global South (Reuters)
Russia to give itself sweeping powers to ban/restrict foreign AI (Reuters)

caveats: Russian benchmark claims are vendor/state-aligned
Nebius (ex-Yandex) relocated out of Russia and is now a Western-market Nvidia partner — not Russian capacity
Actual Russian frontier-compute baseline remains under-measured
claims.recommended_phrasing: Russia is the isolation case: sanctioned out of Nvidia, leaning on Chinese chips and admitting CUDA lock-in, it markets sovereign AI to the Global South and proposed powers to restrict foreign AI tools (March 2026) — values-aligned but, by its own bank's account, slower and not as smart.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Россия — кейс изоляции: отрезанная санкциями от Nvidia, опирающаяся на китайские чипы и признающая lock-in CUDA, она предлагает суверенный AI Global South и (март 2026) предложила полномочия ограничивать иностранные AI-инструменты — ценностно согласовано, но, по словам собственного банка, медленнее и не так умно.

claims.claim: On 7 October 2022 BIS imposed sweeping controls on advanced computing chips (e.g., A100/H100-class) and semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China, the opening move of the chip-control regime.

sources.title: U.S. Export Controls and China (CRS R48642)

caveats: Subsequently updated Oct 2023 and 2024
claims.recommended_phrasing: The chip-control regime begins 7 Oct 2022, when BIS restricted advanced GPUs and chipmaking tools to China (CRS R48642) — the founding act of using the production structure as a security lever.

claims.claim: BIS issued the Framework for AI Diffusion on 15 January 2025 — a worldwide tiered licensing regime covering advanced chips and closed model weights (ECCN 4E091) — set to take effect 15 May 2025.

sources.title: Kirkland & Ellis: BIS Rescission of AI Diffusion Framework
CRS R48642 PDF

caveats: Three country tiers; Tier I exempt, Tier II via data-center VEU, Tier III denied
claims.recommended_phrasing: The Jan 2025 AI Diffusion Rule was the most explicit attempt to formalise a world-tier system — three country tiers governing chip and model-weight access — and the clearest doctrinal statement of AI-as-structural-power.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Правило AI Diffusion (январь 2025) было самой явной попыткой формализовать мировую систему тиров — три страновых тира, регулирующих доступ к чипам и весам моделей — и наиболее чётким доктринальным заявлением об AI как структурной власти.

claims.claim: On 13 May 2025 BIS rescinded the AI Diffusion Rule (two days before effect), promised a replacement, and issued guidance including that Huawei Ascend chips (ECCN 3A090) are presumptively subject to General Prohibition 10.

sources.title: BIS press release: rescission
Hogan Lovells analysis

caveats: Replacement draft was later withdrawn (see export-11)
Rescission timing coincided with Trump's Gulf visit (multiple outlets)
claims.recommended_phrasing: The rescission (13 May 2025) replaced a rules-based tier system with a more discretionary, deal-by-deal regime — leverage exercised case by case — while extraterritorial Huawei Ascend guidance (GP10) extended US reach over third-country chip use.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Отзыв правила (13 мая 2025) заменил основанную на правилах систему тиров на более дискреционный режим по сделкам — рычаг, применяемый точечно — а экстерриториальное руководство по Huawei Ascend (GP10) распространило охват США на использование чипов в третьих странах.

claims.claim: The H20 was effectively banned from China in April 2025 (Nvidia took a ~$4.5-5.5B charge); in August 2025 Nvidia and AMD agreed to remit 15% of China H20/MI308 revenue to the US government in exchange for licenses.

sources.title: Trump says Nvidia will hand the U.S. 15% (NPR)
Nvidia hasn't finalized 15% deal (Yahoo/Nvidia CFO)

caveats: Nvidia CFO said the 15% was a USG expectation not yet codified in regulation; legal basis unclear
FT first reported the arrangement
claims.recommended_phrasing: The clearest rent mechanism: after banning the H20 (April 2025), Washington reportedly agreed in August to let Nvidia/AMD sell to China in exchange for 15% of that revenue — converting export control into a literal toll. Note Nvidia's CFO said the requirement was not yet codified.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Самый явный механизм ренты: после запрета H20 (апрель 2025) Вашингтон, по сообщениям, в августе согласился разрешить Nvidia/AMD продавать в Китай в обмен на 15% этой выручки — превратив экспортный контроль в буквальную пошлину. При этом CFO Nvidia заявил, что требование ещё не кодифицировано.

claims.claim: China escalated critical-mineral controls (gallium/germanium licensing July 2023; Dec 2024 ban on gallium/germanium/antimony to the US; April 2025 and Oct 2025 rare-earth additions), wielding ~98% of refined gallium output as leverage.

sources.title: China Export Controls: Critical Minerals (Andersen Institute)
CRS R48642

caveats: Many controls were partially suspended after Oct 2025 Trump-Xi talks (MOFCOM Announcements 70/72) — a truce, not removal
Antimony exports fell ~97% after Aug 2024 restrictions
claims.recommended_phrasing: Structural power runs both ways: China answered chip controls with mineral controls — gallium/germanium licensing and a Dec 2024 US ban, leveraging ~98% of refined gallium — though most were suspended in a fragile late-2025 truce.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Структурная власть работает в обе стороны: Китай ответил на контроль чипов контролем минералов — лицензирование галлия/германия и запрет на экспорт в США (декабрь 2024), используя ~98% мирового выпуска рафинированного галлия — хотя большинство мер приостановлено в хрупком перемирии конца 2025.

claims.claim: China's SAMR opened an antitrust probe into Nvidia (Dec 2024) and issued a preliminary finding (Sept 2025) that Nvidia violated commitments from its 2020 Mellanox approval — a regulatory pressure lever later suspended in trade talks.

sources.title: CRS R48642 PDF (SAMR Sept 2025 finding)
Beijing presses Washington (Tech Times)

caveats: Widely read as retaliatory leverage rather than genuine competition concern
Status ambiguous after 2025-26 truce; no formal closure notice initially
claims.recommended_phrasing: China also weaponises its own market access: the SAMR antitrust probe into Nvidia (preliminary violation finding Sept 2025) functioned as a bargaining lever, suspended and revived with the trade weather.

claims.claim: The bipartisan GAIN AI Act (S.3150 / H.R.5885, 2025) would require exporters of advanced AI chips to countries of concern to first certify US persons have priority access; it passed the Senate via an NDAA amendment but was dropped from the final defense bill after White House/Nvidia lobbying.

sources.title: S.3150 text (Congress.gov)
Senate Banking GAIN AI Act release

caveats: Standalone bill status pending in committee (inferred); dropped from enacted NDAA per Lawfare
Sponsors: Banks (S), Krishnamoorthi/Moolenaar (H)
corrections: Note standalone status pending and that it was dropped from the enacted NDAA
claims.recommended_phrasing: Congress is institutionalising the logic: the GAIN AI Act (S.3150/H.R.5885, 2025) would force chipmakers to serve US buyers before exporting to adversaries — it cleared the Senate in the NDAA but was stripped from the final bill after White House and Nvidia lobbying.

claims.claim: The Chip Security Act (H.R.3447 / S.1705, introduced May 2025) would require advanced AI chips to carry location-verification mechanisms before export; it passed House Foreign Affairs Committee 42-0.

sources.title: H.R.3447 text (Congress.gov)
House Select Committee: Chip Security Act passage

caveats: Sponsors Huizenga/Foster (H), Cotton (S); committee vote occurred March 2026
Pending full House floor
claims.recommended_phrasing: The Chip Security Act (H.R.3447, 2025) would mandate location-tracking on exported AI chips — turning silicon into a surveilled, conditionally-licensed asset — and cleared House Foreign Affairs 42-0.

claims.claim: The AI OVERWATCH Act (H.R.6875, introduced Dec 2025) would require licenses and a 30-day congressional-review window (modeled on the Arms Export Control Act) for advanced-AI-chip exports to six countries of concern; it advanced from House Foreign Affairs 42-2 in Jan 2026.

sources.title: H.R.6875 titles (Congress.gov)
HFAC: Chairman Mast introduces AI OVERWATCH Act

caveats: Committee tally cited as 42-2 (Lawfare) or 42-2-1 (other)
Opposed by White House (Sacks) and Nvidia
claims.recommended_phrasing: The AI OVERWATCH Act (H.R.6875, Dec 2025) would put advanced-chip exports under arms-control-style congressional review for six adversary states — explicitly treating compute like a weapons system — and advanced from committee 42-2.

claims.claim: The US has repeatedly forced ownership divorces as a condition of stack participation (TikTok divestiture law; G42 divesting ByteDance and Huawei), establishing that a firm cannot be in both China's camp and ours (Raimondo).

sources.title: Microsoft and U.S. join AI forces in UAE deal (Raimondo quote)

caveats: TikTok enforcement has been politically irregular under the current administration
claims.recommended_phrasing: A recurring discipline mechanism is the forced divorce — G42 shedding ByteDance/Huawei, the TikTok divestiture law — codifying Raimondo's line that a firm cannot be both in China's camp and ours.

claims.claim: On 13 March 2026 the US Commerce Department withdrew its planned replacement rule for AI chip exports, leaving pre-existing controls and case-by-case guidance in force and the codified tier architecture unresolved.

sources.title: US Commerce withdraws planned rule on AI chip exports (Reuters)

caveats: No successor rule published as of mid-2026; track any future rule
Withdrawal means the export regime is currently discretionary, not a stable codified tier order
claims.recommended_phrasing: The tier order is doctrine in flux, not settled architecture: after rescinding the AI Diffusion Rule, Commerce withdrew its draft replacement (March 2026), so the regime now runs on pre-existing controls and discretionary guidance — phrase the world-tier system as an active project, not a finished order.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Система тиров — это доктрина в движении, а не устоявшаяся архитектура: отозвав правило AI Diffusion, Минторг США снял и проект замены (март 2026), так что режим теперь работает на ранее действующих ограничениях и дискреционном руководстве — формулируйте мировую систему тиров как активный проект, а не завершённый порядок.

claims.claim: BIS confirmed (31 May 2026) that a license is required to export advanced computing items to entities headquartered in Country Group D:5 or Macau, even when those entities operate abroad — control follows ultimate parentage, not only physical destination.

sources.title: BIS guidance May 31, 2026 (PDF)

caveats: Add examples of enforcement or denied licenses if available
claims.recommended_phrasing: US reach extends by ownership, not geography: BIS confirmed (May 2026) that exporting advanced compute to D:5/Macau-headquartered entities requires a license wherever they operate — the panopticon follows the parent company, the strongest current evidence of extraterritorial stack control.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Охват США определяется собственностью, а не географией: BIS подтвердил (май 2026), что экспорт продвинутого compute структурам со штаб-квартирой в D:5/Макао требует лицензии независимо от места их работы — паноптикум следует за материнской компанией, сильнейшее текущее свидетельство экстерриториального контроля над стеком.

claims.claim: At DARPA's AIxCC final (DEF CON 33, Aug 2025), autonomous cyber reasoning systems found a majority of synthetic vulnerabilities and patched most of them, also surfacing 18 real-world vulnerabilities; tools were open-sourced. DARPA reported 54/63 found (86%) and 68% patched; Axios reported 77% found / 61% patched / ~45-min average patch on a 70-bug set.

sources.title: AIxCC marks pivotal inflection point (DARPA)
Cybersecurity Dive: DARPA touts results
Axios Future of Cybersecurity (alt figures)

caveats: Synthetic, time-boxed (4 hrs cloud compute) on tens of millions of lines of code — not equivalent to real-world adversarial ops
Headline number is for DEFENSE (find-and-fix), not autonomous offense
Found/patched rates differ by source (DARPA 86%/68% vs Axios 77%/61%) due to different denominators; prefer DARPA primary
corrections: For slides use DARPA primary figures (86% found / 68% patched); the 45-min average patch time is a useful Axios add-on
claims.recommended_phrasing: The proven, replicable datapoint is defensive: AIxCC systems autonomously found the large majority of seeded bugs (86% per DARPA), patched most (68%) and surfaced 18 real ones (Aug 2025) — strong evidence that automated find-and-fix is real, and that defense can scale.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Доказанный воспроизводимый факт — оборонительный: системы AIxCC автономно нашли подавляющее большинство заложенных багов (86% по DARPA), пропатчили большую часть (68%) и обнаружили 18 реальных (август 2025) — сильное свидетельство, что автоматический find-and-fix реален и что оборона масштабируется.

claims.claim: Anthropic reported (Nov 2025) a Chinese state-linked actor (GTG-1002) used Claude Code to run a largely autonomous (80-90%) cyber-espionage campaign against ~30 organisations — described as the first such case at scale.

sources.title: Anthropic claims met with doubt (BleepingComputer)
What we know and what we're watching (SC Media)

caveats: No IoCs published; attribution methodology undisclosed; no government corroboration
Anthropic admits Claude hallucinated/overstated findings; researchers (Beaumont, Kirk) called it overstated
Vendor has commercial incentive — D-level
corrections: Do NOT state as established fact that AI executed an autonomous attack at scale — it is a contested vendor disclosure
claims.recommended_phrasing: Vendor and lab claims suggest AI is accelerating offensive cyber: Anthropic's GTG-1002 disclosure (Nov 2025) describes a largely AI-run espionage campaign — but it lacks IoCs and independent corroboration and Anthropic concedes the model hallucinated, so treat it as a stress-test scenario, not proof of reliable exploit-at-scale.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Заявления вендоров и лабораторий указывают, что AI ускоряет наступательный кибер: раскрытие GTG-1002 от Anthropic (ноябрь 2025) описывает в основном AI-управляемую шпионскую кампанию — но без IoC и независимого подтверждения, и Anthropic признаёт галлюцинации модели, поэтому это стресс-тест-сценарий, а не доказательство надёжного exploit-at-scale.

claims.claim: Google's Big Sleep agent (DeepMind + Project Zero) found the first AI-discovered real-world exploitable bug (SQLite, Nov 2024), prevented an in-the-wild SQLite zero-day (CVE-2025-6965, July 2025), and reported its first 20 vulnerabilities (Aug 2025).

sources.title: From Naptime to Big Sleep (Project Zero)
Big Sleep agent makes big leap (Google Cloud)

caveats: Human expert in loop for final review before reporting
Demonstrates the same capability cuts BOTH ways — defense benefits too
claims.recommended_phrasing: Automated vulnerability discovery is genuinely advancing on the defensive side: Google's Big Sleep found a real SQLite bug (Nov 2024), foiled an in-the-wild zero-day (July 2025) and reported 20 vulnerabilities (Aug 2025) — confirming the capability is real but also that defenders wield it.

claims.claim: Because AIxCC tools were open-sourced and Big Sleep operates defensively, the offense-dominance thesis is contested: defensive automation (autonomous patching, virtual patching) is scaling in parallel.

sources.title: AIxCC finals winners (DARPA)

caveats: Net offense-defense balance is genuinely uncertain and actively debated
corrections: Drop any offense-dominance phrasing; argue from access asymmetry instead
claims.recommended_phrasing: The honest framing is symmetry, not offense-dominance: the same AIxCC systems that find bugs were open-sourced for defenders, so the structural-power claim should rest on access asymmetry (who has the tools), not on a settled belief that AI favours attackers.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Честная рамка — симметрия, а не доминирование атаки: те же системы AIxCC, что находят баги, выложены в открытый доступ для защитников, поэтому тезис о структурной власти должен опираться на асимметрию доступа (у кого есть инструменты), а не на убеждение, что AI выгоден атакующим.

claims.claim: OSS-CRS (arXiv, March 2026) ported AIxCC-style cyber reasoning techniques to real open-source projects and discovered 10 previously unknown bugs (3 high-severity) across 8 OSS-Fuzz projects — evidence competition systems transfer to real targets.

sources.title: OSS-CRS: porting AIxCC CRS to real-world OSS (arXiv)

caveats: Preprint; needs peer review, independent replication and a CVE/patch trail
Defensive/research framing, not offensive operations
claims.recommended_phrasing: The find-and-fix capability transfers beyond the contest: OSS-CRS (March 2026) applied AIxCC techniques to real open-source code and found 10 unknown bugs (3 high-severity) — a preprint result, but it shows competition tooling reaching real targets, again on the defensive side.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Способность find-and-fix переносится за пределы соревнования: OSS-CRS (март 2026) применил техники AIxCC к реальному open-source коду и нашёл 10 неизвестных багов (3 high-severity) — это препринт, но он показывает, что соревновательный инструментарий доходит до реальных целей, снова на стороне обороны.

claims.claim: Anthropic, the UK AI Security Institute and the Alan Turing Institute found (Oct 2025) that as few as ~250 malicious documents (~0.00016% of training data) can backdoor LLMs from 600M to 13B parameters, regardless of model size.

sources.title: A small number of samples can poison LLMs (Anthropic)
Alan Turing Institute summary

caveats: Tested narrow denial-of-service gibberish backdoor; unclear if it generalises to dangerous behaviours
Pretraining-from-scratch experimental setup
claims.recommended_phrasing: The knowledge structure has a concrete attack surface: ~250 poisoned documents (0.00016% of data) can backdoor models regardless of size (Anthropic/UK AISI/Alan Turing, Oct 2025) — though the demonstrated backdoor is narrow, so do not overstate to models can be weaponised at will.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: У структуры знания есть конкретная поверхность атаки: ~250 отравленных документов (0.00016% данных) могут внедрить backdoor в модель независимо от размера (Anthropic/UK AISI/Alan Turing, октябрь 2025) — хотя продемонстрированный backdoor узкий, поэтому не преувеличивайте до модель можно превратить в оружие по желанию.

claims.claim: Prompt injection (direct and indirect) is the #1 risk in the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (2025) — LLM01 — and OWASP states it cannot be fully patched because it exploits the LLM design of mixing instructions and data.

sources.title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025 (PDF)

caveats: OWASP list not ranked by real-world exploitation frequency
claims.recommended_phrasing: Indirect prompt injection — hidden instructions in documents/RAG content a model treats as trusted — is OWASP's top LLM risk (LLM01, 2025) and structurally hard to eliminate, the technical core of the cognitive-influence-inside-decision-support argument.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Непрямая prompt injection — скрытые инструкции в документах/RAG-контенте, которые модель считает доверенными — это риск №1 в OWASP для LLM (LLM01, 2025) и структурно трудно устраним, технологическое ядро аргумента о когнитивном влиянии внутри decision-support.

claims.claim: In July 2025 the DoD CDAO awarded contracts with a $200M ceiling each to Anthropic, Google, OpenAI and xAI to build agentic AI workflows for national-security missions; Anthropic's Claude is integrated via Palantir into classified workflows.

sources.title: Anthropic awarded $200M DOD agreement
Breaking Defense: $200M each for agentic AI

caveats: $200M is a ceiling, not guaranteed spend
Embedding does not equal demonstrated compromise; the risk is structural exposure
claims.recommended_phrasing: AI decision-support is being embedded at the core of the security structure: the DoD awarded up to $200M each to Anthropic, Google, OpenAI and xAI (July 2025) for agentic workflows, with Claude running in classified workflows via Palantir — making model and data provenance a national-security supply-chain question.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: AI-decision-support встраивается в ядро структуры безопасности: Минобороны США выделило по $200 млн (потолок) Anthropic, Google, OpenAI и xAI (июль 2025) на агентные процессы, а Claude работает в секретных процессах через Palantir — что делает провенанс моделей и данных вопросом национальной supply-chain безопасности.

claims.claim: Cognitive-security risks via AI (bias, misinformation, supply-chain poisoning) are partly continuous with older software-supply-chain and influence-operation problems, not wholly new.

sources.title: OWASP Top 10 LLM 2025 (PDF)

caveats: Author should pre-empt the nothing-new objection
claims.recommended_phrasing: Be honest about novelty: poisoning and influence are extensions of known software-supply-chain and FIMI problems — what is new is scale, autonomy and the concentration of the channel inside decision-support, not the existence of the risk.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Честно о новизне: отравление и влияние — это продолжение известных проблем software-supply-chain и FIMI; новое — масштаб, автономность и концентрация канала внутри decision-support, а не само наличие риска.

claims.topic: Indirect prompt injection in the wild
claims.claim: A large-scale study (arXiv, April 2026) analysing 1.2B URLs across 24.8M hosts found 15,300 validated indirect-prompt-injection instances across 11,700 pages, with ~70% in non-rendered HTML — evidence external web data is already a machine-facing influence channel for agents/RAG.

sources.title: Indirect prompt injections in the wild at web scale (arXiv)

caveats: Preprint; prevalence study, not a confirmed enterprise/government incident
Validated instances are a lower bound of what exists in the wild
claims.recommended_phrasing: Cognitive influence is no longer hypothetical at the data layer: a web-scale study (April 2026) validated 15.3K indirect-prompt-injection instances across 11.7K pages out of 1.2B URLs — moving the risk from lab demo to measured in-the-wild prevalence, though a confirmed decision-support incident is still the missing piece.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Когнитивное влияние больше не гипотетично на уровне данных: исследование веб-масштаба (апрель 2026) подтвердило 15.3 тыс. инстансов непрямой prompt injection на 11.7 тыс. страниц из 1.2 млрд URL — переводя риск из лабораторного демо в измеренную распространённость в дикой природе, хотя подтверждённого инцидента в decision-support всё ещё не хватает.

claims.claim: A real-world study (arXiv, May 2026) of ~200,000 resumes found hidden prompt injections in approximately 1% of them, with >90% using non-explicit instructions — evidence prompt injection has entered deployed AI-mediated evaluation (hiring) workflows.

sources.title: Hidden prompt injections in resume screening (arXiv)

caveats: Preprint; needs hiring-platform incident reports and mitigations
1% is the share containing injections, not the share that succeeded
claims.recommended_phrasing: The influence surface has reached a real decision workflow: ~1% of ~200K resumes carried hidden prompt injections aimed at LLM screeners (May 2026), most using non-explicit instructions — concrete evidence that AI-mediated evaluation is already being gamed at the input layer.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Поверхность влияния дошла до реального decision-workflow: ~1% из ~200 тыс. резюме содержали скрытые prompt injection, нацеленные на LLM-скрининг (май 2026), большинство — неявные инструкции — конкретное свидетельство, что AI-оценка уже подвергается манипуляции на входном уровне.

claims.claim: A public red-team competition (arXiv, March 2026) tested 13 frontier AI agents with ~272,000 attack attempts and recorded 8,648 successful indirect-prompt-injection attacks — quantifying that agentic systems remain structurally vulnerable to hidden-instruction attacks.

sources.title: Red-teaming frontier agents for indirect prompt injection (arXiv)

caveats: Preprint/competition; not production-incident evidence
Success rate depends on attack-surface assumptions in the competition design
claims.recommended_phrasing: Agent vulnerability is now quantified, not asserted: a red-team competition (March 2026) logged 8,648 successful indirect-prompt-injection attacks against 13 frontier agents over ~272K attempts — strong evidence the structural weakness is real, pending production-incident confirmation.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Уязвимость агентов теперь измерена, а не просто заявлена: red-team-соревнование (март 2026) зафиксировало 8 648 успешных атак непрямой prompt injection против 13 frontier-агентов на ~272 тыс. попыток — сильное свидетельство реальности структурной слабости, в ожидании подтверждения production-инцидентом.

claims.claim: The AI Agent Index (arXiv, Feb 2026) documented 30 deployed state-of-the-art agentic AI systems and found uneven safety/evaluation transparency — agents are deployed faster than documentation and safety transparency mature.

sources.title: The AI Agent Index (arXiv)

caveats: Snapshot; update with live index data before final publication
Documents transparency gaps, not specific incidents
claims.recommended_phrasing: Deployment is outrunning oversight: the AI Agent Index (Feb 2026) catalogued 30 deployed agentic systems with uneven safety transparency — supporting the call for an agent registry and provenance controls, especially for government and critical-infrastructure agents.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Развёртывание обгоняет надзор: AI Agent Index (февраль 2026) каталогизировал 30 развёрнутых агентных систем с неравномерной прозрачностью безопасности — что подкрепляет необходимость реестра агентов и контроля провенанса, особенно для государственных и критически важных агентов.

claims.claim: DeepSeek's open-weight R1 (Jan 2025) matched frontier reasoning at far lower cost and triggered a ~$589B single-day drop in Nvidia's market cap (27 Jan 2025), the largest in US stock-market history.

sources.title: DeepSeek, Huawei, Export Controls (CSIS)
DeepSeek V4 (CNBC, recaps R1 impact)

caveats: DeepSeek's ~$6M training cost is a reported/contested figure
R1 used Nvidia chips — open weights does not equal chip independence
claims.recommended_phrasing: Open weights create a real exit at the model-artifact layer: DeepSeek R1 (Jan 2025) matched frontier reasoning cheaply and erased ~$589B of Nvidia value in a day — but it was trained on Nvidia silicon, so it changed the model economics, not the hardware dependency.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Открытые веса создают реальный exit на уровне модели-артефакта: DeepSeek R1 (январь 2025) сравнялся с frontier-рассуждением дёшево и стёр ~$589 млрд капитализации Nvidia за день — но обучался на кремнии Nvidia, так что изменил экономику моделей, а не зависимость от железа.

claims.claim: Open weights do not automatically transfer learning curves, chips, cloud or operational tempo: DeepSeek reportedly reverted R2 to Nvidia after Ascend instability, and SMIC ~30-50% 7nm yields constrain domestic chip scaling.

sources.title: How China's GLM-5 Works (SMIC yield, R2 reversion)
Huawei Ascend Production Ramp (SemiAnalysis)

caveats: R2/Ascend reversion is reported, not officially confirmed
Yield figures are analyst estimates
claims.recommended_phrasing: The key test is exit vs change-of-metropole: open weights move the model layer, but tacit iteration, HBM, SMIC yields (~30-50% at 7nm) and ecosystem depth do not transfer — so sovereign AI on foreign chips is a change of dependency, not an exit.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Ключевой тест — exit против смены метрополии: открытые веса двигают слой моделей, но неявная итерация, HBM, выход SMIC (~30-50% на 7нм) и глубина экосистемы не переносятся — поэтому суверенный AI на чужих чипах — это смена зависимости, а не exit.

claims.claim: DeepSeek's open-weight V4 (April 2026) launched with Huawei Ascend full support and gave Ascend early optimisation access ahead of Nvidia/AMD, with SMIC shares rising ~10% on the news — evidence the Chinese stack is moving toward serving (and partly training) on domestic silicon.

sources.title: China's DeepSeek releases V4 (CNBC)

caveats: Extent of Ascend use in TRAINING (vs inference) remains unclear (CNBC)
Marketing dimension to full-support claims
corrections: State that training-vs-inference Ascend share is unconfirmed
claims.recommended_phrasing: The trajectory is real but unfinished: DeepSeek V4 (April 2026) shipped with Huawei Ascend full support and priority optimisation, but how much training (vs inference) ran on Ascend is unconfirmed — serving independence is closer than training independence.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Траектория реальна, но не завершена: DeepSeek V4 (апрель 2026) вышел с полной поддержкой Huawei Ascend и приоритетной оптимизацией, но сколько обучения (против инференса) шло на Ascend — не подтверждено: независимость по инференсу ближе, чем по обучению.

claims.claim: Most national sovereign AI programmes (India, UK, Gulf, EU, Norway) are built on Nvidia chips, US/foreign cloud and US/Chinese models — the dominant pattern is sovereign branding atop foreign foundations.

sources.title: Built for Purpose? EU AI Gigafactories (Interface)
India Fuels Its AI Mission With NVIDIA

caveats: ~95% of commercially available AI compute is US/China-operated (Hawkins et al.)
claims.recommended_phrasing: Across India, the UK, the Gulf, Norway and the EU, sovereign AI overwhelmingly means sovereign branding on foreign foundations — Nvidia chips, foreign cloud, US/Chinese models — which is exactly the tiered-dependence the structural-power thesis predicts.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: В Индии, UK, Заливе, Норвегии и ЕС суверенный AI в подавляющем большинстве означает суверенный брендинг на чужом фундаменте — чипы Nvidia, иностранное облако, модели США/Китая — ровно та тиерная зависимость, которую предсказывает тезис о структурной власти.

claims.claim: Stanford AI Index 2026 primary figures: global private AI investment $344.7B in 2025 (+127.5% YoY), global corporate AI investment $581.7B (+130%), US private $285.9B vs China $12.4B (23.1x); US 50 notable models vs China 30; US researcher inflow -89% since 2017.

sources.title: Inside the AI Index: 12 Takeaways (Stanford HAI)
The 2026 AI Index Report (Stanford HAI)

caveats: Investment figures are private/corporate as tracked; understate China per Stanford
Notable-model counts use Stanford's notable-model methodology
claims.recommended_phrasing: Per the Stanford AI Index 2026 primary tables: global private AI investment hit $344.7B in 2025 (+127.5%) and corporate AI investment $581.7B (+130%), with the US at $285.9B vs China's $12.4B (23.1x) and the US producing 50 notable models to China's 30 — concentration is extreme, but Stanford flags the private figure understates China.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: По первичным таблицам Stanford AI Index 2026: глобальные частные инвестиции в AI достигли $344.7 млрд в 2025 (+127.5%), корпоративные — $581.7 млрд (+130%); США $285.9 млрд против $12.4 млрд у Китая (23.1x); США выпустили 50 заметных моделей против 30 у Китая — концентрация крайняя, но Стэнфорд отмечает, что частная цифра занижает Китай.

claims.claim: On 12 June 2026 the US Commerce Department (Secretary Lutnick) issued an export-control directive requiring Anthropic to suspend all access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national inside or outside the US (including foreign-national employees), with a license now required to export, re-export or domestically transfer those models; Anthropic disabled both globally to comply and disputes the basis.
