sources.title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 (Anthropic)
Anthropic Says US Orders Halt to Foreign Access for Fable 5, Mythos 5 (Bloomberg)
Trump admin blocks foreign access to Anthropic's most powerful AI (Axios)

caveats: Directive received 5:21pm ET June 12; govt has not published the order text (GAP on primary FR/license)
Trigger reported as a third-party jailbreak claim; per WSJ, Amazon CEO Jassy raised concerns to the White House; Semafor reported fears of China-linked access — none independently confirmed
Anthropic says it was given only verbal evidence of a narrow jailbreak and that the capability is widely available (e.g., GPT-5.5)
claims.recommended_phrasing: The clearest evidence that export controls have reached the knowledge layer: on 12 June 2026 Commerce ordered Anthropic to bar all foreign-national access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 (license now required to export/transfer the models), and Anthropic disabled them worldwide to comply while disputing the basis — a frontier model treated as a controlled munition.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Самое явное свидетельство, что экспортный контроль дошёл до слоя знания: 12 июня 2026 Минторг США обязал Anthropic заблокировать доступ к Fable 5 и Mythos 5 для всех иностранцев (теперь нужна лицензия на экспорт/передачу моделей), и Anthropic отключил их по всему миру, оспаривая основание — frontier-модель как контролируемое вооружение.

claims.topic: DoD supply-chain-risk label + Anthropic lawsuit + Mythos cleared to ~100 orgs
claims.claim: After negotiations collapsed, the DoD declared Anthropic a 'supply chain risk' (a label historically reserved for foreign adversaries, requiring defense contractors to certify they will not use Claude in military work); Anthropic sued the administration to reverse the blacklisting; on 26 June 2026 Commerce granted Anthropic permission to release Mythos 5 to roughly 100 vetted US companies and federal agencies, but did not restore Fable 5.

sources.title: Trump admin allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI to some companies, agencies (CNBC)
Anthropic's Mythos Recall and the White House's Missing AI Safety Playbook (TechPolicy.Press)

caveats: Litigation ongoing; outcome unknown
Negotiations led by co-founder Tom Brown (replacing Amodei); fast-moving
claims.recommended_phrasing: The state exercised raw control over a private firm's flagship product: the DoD branded Anthropic a 'supply-chain risk' (a label normally for foreign adversaries) and barred its models from military work, Anthropic sued, and Commerce then cleared Mythos 5 for ~100 vetted US organisations (June 26) while keeping Fable 5 dark — control via directive and procurement, not ownership.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Государство применило прямой контроль над флагманским продуктом частной компании: DoD объявил Anthropic 'supply-chain risk' (ярлык обычно для иностранных противников) и запретил её модели в военной работе, Anthropic подал в суд, а затем Минторг разрешил Mythos 5 для ~100 проверенных организаций США (26 июня), оставив Fable 5 отключённым — контроль через директиву и закупки, а не собственность.

claims.topic: 2026 chip toll-and-throttle regime (closes the chip-status gap)
claims.claim: By 2026 the chip regime is graduated, not a wall: BIS (15 Jan 2026, FR 2026-00789) shifted H200 and AMD MI325X to case-by-case review with a 25% tariff, 50% volume cap, third-party testing, KYC and US inspection; Blackwell (B200/GB200/GB300) remains presumption-of-denial; H20 sales were re-permitted under the 15% arrangement; Huawei plans to ship 600,000 Ascend 910C in 2026; Beijing remains ambivalent about importing.

sources.title: BIS revises license review policy for semiconductors to China (bis.gov)
Nvidia gets US license for small amount of H200 exports to China (Bloomberg)

caveats: Whether Beijing approves H200 imports is unresolved (China discouraged purchases, as with H20)
Blackwell denial could be hardened by the AI OVERWATCH Act (2-yr statutory ban) if enacted
Huawei shipment numbers are targets, not verified output
corrections: Replace 'advanced chips are banned from China' with the graduated toll-and-throttle description
claims.recommended_phrasing: By 2026 the chip lever is a toll-and-throttle regime, not a wall: BIS put H200 and MI325X on case-by-case review (25% tariff, 50% cap, KYC, US inspection), Blackwell stays denied, and the H20 flows again under the 15% arrangement — while Beijing throttles from its side and Huawei targets 600,000 Ascend 910C in 2026. State export control now meters access by generation, price and surveillance.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: К 2026 чиповый рычаг — это режим 'вентиль с пошлиной', а не стена: BIS перевёл H200 и MI325X на case-by-case (25% пошлина, 50% потолок, KYC, инспекция в США), Blackwell остаётся под denial, а H20 снова идёт по схеме с 15% — при этом Пекин дросселирует со своей стороны, а Huawei целится в 600 000 Ascend 910C в 2026. Госэкспортконтроль теперь дозирует доступ по поколению, цене и слежке.

claims.claim: At ISC.AI 2026 (24 June), Qihoo 360 (US Entity List since 2020) unveiled 'Yitian Tulong' (Tulongfeng for discovery, Yitianzhen for defense), claiming 'Mythos-equivalent' capability via a multi-agent swarm and 3,432 vulnerabilities found including 105 confirmed by Chinese authorities; Reuters could not independently verify the claims and 360 published no technical evidence.

sources.title: China's 360 says it has developed tools to match Anthropic's Mythos (Reuters)
Chinese cybersecurity company claims a better-than-Mythos bug finder (The Register)

caveats: Vendor capability claim; Reuters explicitly could not verify; no technical evidence published
360 is on the US Entity List (2020) for alleged military links; CVERC amplifies its research
Zhou acknowledges Chinese frontier models lag US by 20-30%
corrections: Do NOT state as fact that China has a Mythos-equivalent; it is an unverified vendor claim
claims.recommended_phrasing: China's counter-claim is loud but unverified: Qihoo 360 says its Tulongfeng swarm matches Mythos and found 3,432 bugs (105 confirmed by Chinese authorities), but Reuters could not verify it and 360 published no technical evidence — treat as a disputed vendor claim that signals strategic intent (and anxiety about 'one-way transparency'), not a demonstrated capability.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Китайский контр-ответ громкий, но неподтверждённый: Qihoo 360 заявляет, что её рой Tulongfeng сравним с Mythos и нашёл 3 432 бага (105 подтверждены китайскими властями), но Reuters не смог это проверить, а 360 не опубликовала технических доказательств — это спорное заявление вендора, сигнализирующее стратегическое намерение (и тревогу о 'one-way transparency'), а не продемонстрированную способность.

claims.topic: The weapon is access asymmetry, not raw capability
claims.claim: The structural-power core of the Mythos episode is access asymmetry, not a settled capability leap: Mythos is restricted to ~40 vetted organisations under Project Glasswing and US-person-only after the June directive, so opponents frame it as a 'cyber nuclear weapon' because of the access ban (one-way transparency), while Anthropic's own review found the cited jailbreak surfaced only minor, previously-known vulnerabilities and that comparable capability is widely available (e.g., GPT-5.5).

sources.title: Statement on the US directive (Anthropic, capability-widely-available point)
China's 360 says it has developed tools to match Mythos (Reuters; 'one-way transparency' framing)

caveats: Both sides have incentives: Anthropic downplays risk; US/360 amplify it
Mythos-class autonomous exploit-chaining is an Anthropic disclosure, not independently benchmarked (see V4 rediscovery counter-preprints)
corrections: Frame the leverage as WHO can access the capability under what conditions, not as 'AI now finds 0-days at will'
claims.recommended_phrasing: The lever here is access, not magic: Mythos is gated to ~40 Glasswing organisations and US persons, which is precisely why rivals call it a 'cyber nuclear weapon' — a one-way-transparency advantage — even as Anthropic argues the specific flagged jailbreak found only minor known bugs and similar capability exists elsewhere. The structural-power claim should rest on controlled, asymmetric access, not on a settled machine-speed-0day fact.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Рычаг здесь — доступ, а не магия: Mythos закрыт для ~40 организаций Glasswing и лиц США, и именно поэтому соперники называют его 'кибер-ядерным оружием' — преимущество one-way transparency — хотя Anthropic утверждает, что конкретный jailbreak нашёл лишь мелкие известные баги, а сопоставимая способность есть и у других. Тезис о структурной власти должен опираться на контролируемый, асимметричный доступ, а не на 'устоявшийся факт' machine-speed 0-day.

claims.topic: Legal instrument and contested basis of the model-export directive
claims.claim: The June 12 2026 action was a BIS 'is-informed' letter (signed by Secretary Lutnick) invoking ECRA 50 U.S.C. 4817(b)(1) and EAR 15 C.F.R. 744.22(b) to require a validated license for any foreign-national access to Mythos 5 / Fable 5 — the first such use against an AI model; the government has not published the order, and multiple legal experts argue the basis is shaky (SaaS is not an EAR 'item', the worldwide scope exceeds 744.22's country list, First Amendment concerns), while 80+ security executives and G7 governments urged restoration.

sources.title: Read the Lutnick Letter That Led Anthropic to Disable Mythos (Bloomberg)
A Kill Switch for Frontier AI (Lawfare)
Commerce Restricted Access to Anthropic's Models: What Comes Next? (CSIS)

caveats: EVENT verified (Bloomberg has the letter; authorities identified); LEGAL VALIDITY is contested and untested in court
Government has not published the official order text — primary gov document is a gap
CSIS notes the AI Diffusion Rule already covered model weights but is unenforced since May 2025, so this one-off 'is-informed' letter is the workaround
corrections: Do not state the legal basis is settled; phrase as 'contested' / 'untested'
claims.recommended_phrasing: The mechanism is a BIS 'is-informed' letter under ECRA/EAR military-intelligence authorities — the first applied to an AI model — but its validity is openly contested (services arguably fall outside export law, the worldwide scope exceeds the statute's country list, and there are free-speech concerns), and 80+ security leaders plus G7 governments pushed back. Use it as evidence of the state reaching for export-control law to govern models, while flagging the legal fragility.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Механизм — это 'is-informed' письмо BIS на основании ECRA/EAR (military-intelligence) — впервые применённое к AI-модели — но его валидность открыто оспаривается (услуги, вероятно, вне экспортного права; всемирный охват шире странового списка закона; вопросы свободы слова), и 80+ лидеров безопасности плюс правительства G7 выступили против. Используйте как свидетельство того, что государство тянется к экспортному праву для управления моделями, отмечая правовую хрупкость.

claims.topic: Palantir as consolidated decision-OS (primary contract record)
claims.claim: On 31 July 2025 the U.S. Army awarded Palantir an Enterprise Agreement (contract W519TC-25-D-0039) consolidating 75 contracts (15 prime + 60 related) into one 10-year vehicle with a $10B ceiling — but with $0 obligated at award; the Army states the $10B is 'the maximum potential value ... not any specific obligations', positioning Palantir as the Army's consolidated software/data backbone.

sources.title: U.S. Army Awards Enterprise Service Agreement (army.mil)
Army plans big shakeup; $10B Palantir deal (DefenseScoop)

caveats: $10B is a CEILING; action obligation was $0 at award — do not report as money spent
IDIQ/Enterprise Agreement; actual spend depends on task orders over 10 years
Single bidder (sole-source transition of existing Palantir prime/sub contracts)
corrections: Cite as 'up to $10B ceiling, $0 obligated at award', not '$10B awarded/spent'
claims.recommended_phrasing: Palantir is being made the Army's consolidated decision-and-data backbone: a single Enterprise Agreement (W519TC-25-D-0039) folded 75 contracts into one 10-year vehicle with a $10B ceiling — though $0 was obligated at award, so treat the $10B as maximum potential value, not spend. The structural point is consolidation and lock-in, not the headline number.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Palantir делают консолидированным decision-and-data-бэкбоном Армии: одно Enterprise Agreement (W519TC-25-D-0039) свернуло 75 контрактов в один 10-летний механизм с потолком $10 млрд — но на момент награждения обязано $0, так что $10 млрд — это максимальный потенциал, а не расход. Структурный смысл — консолидация и lock-in, а не громкая цифра.

claims.claim: On 5 June 2026 Malaysian customs seized 72 servers containing advanced AI chips (RM52.9M / ~$12.93M) at the KLIA free-trade zone, declared as 'computer components' and destined for re-export to another Asian country; the probe runs under Malaysia's Strategic Trade Act 2010, after Malaysia imposed controls on US-origin high-performance chips in 2025 under US pressure.

sources.title: Malaysia customs seizes AI chips worth $13 million at Kuala Lumpur airport (Reuters)
Customs Dept seizes 72 servers with AI chips worth RM53mil (The Star)

caveats: A single seizure proves attempts and enforcement, not total leakage volume
Chip model, final destination, alleged US-export-control nexus and prosecution outcome not yet public
corrections: Use as evidence of BOTH leakage and policing; do not claim controls are airtight
claims.recommended_phrasing: Controls leak but also police: Malaysia seized 72 AI-chip servers (~$12.93M) at KLIA in June 2026, declared as 'computer components' for onward re-export — concrete proof that transit states are being folded into the enforcement layer. The right formula is that structural power is cost, delay, risk and surveillance, not perfect denial; leverage does not have to be airtight to be structural.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Контроль протекает, но и полицирует: в июне 2026 Малайзия изъяла 72 сервера с AI-чипами (~$12.93 млн) в KLIA, задекларированных как 'computer components' под дальнейший re-export — конкретное доказательство, что транзитные страны встраиваются в слой enforcement. Правильная формула: структурная власть — это цена, задержка, риск и слежка, а не идеальный запрет; leverage не обязан быть герметичным, чтобы быть структурным.

claims.topic: Energy/grid as bottom stack layer (real but region-specific, partly flexible)
claims.claim: AI data-center load is a structural energy/grid layer: modeling projects the six leading firms rising from ~118 TWh (2024) to 239-295 TWh by 2030 with regional power-system stress, yet real-world work shows GPU clusters can curtail/shift workloads (a 130 kW cluster demonstrated grid-responsiveness) and load flexibility can cut grid investment/operating costs 3-21% — so the bottleneck is real but location-dependent and partly manageable.

sources.title: AI data-center concentrated siting and power-system stress (arXiv 2604.06198)
Power-flexible AI data centers, 130 kW real-world cluster (arXiv 2606.25098)
AI load flexibility reduces grid cost 3-21% (arXiv 2604.05376)

caveats: All four supporting studies are preprints pending peer review
Flexibility does not always reduce required generation capacity; benefits diminish and are region-specific
Cross-check projections with IEA/EPRI/NERC and utility disclosures
corrections: Do not frame energy as an absolute, uniform bottleneck; frame as local and software-manageable
claims.recommended_phrasing: Energy is the bottom of the stack and a real constraint — six leading firms' electricity use is modeled to roughly double-to-triple to 239-295 TWh by 2030 with local grid stress — but it is not absolute destiny: GPU clusters can be made grid-responsive (curtail/shift load), trimming grid costs 3-21% in some settings. Frame energy as a structural layer whose constraint is regional and partly software-manageable, not a uniform wall.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Энергия — это нижний слой стека и реальное ограничение: потребление шести ведущих фирм по модели вырастает примерно в 2-2.5 раза до 239-295 ТВт·ч к 2030 с локальным grid stress — но это не абсолютная судьба: GPU-кластеры можно сделать grid-responsive (curtail/shift нагрузки), срезая затраты сети на 3-21% в части случаев. Формулируйте энергию как структурный слой, чьё ограничение регионально и частично управляемо софтом, а не как однородную стену.

claims.claim: AI infrastructure now has a visible political surface: an Axios/Milltown poll (June 2026, ~6,872 respondents) found data-center backlash functioning as a proxy for broader AI anxiety, with roughly half of respondents supporting a temporary ban on new data-center construction.

sources.title: Axios/Milltown poll: AI data-center backlash (Axios)

caveats: Backlash may be a proxy for broader AI anxiety, complicating causal claims about local data-center impacts
Full crosstabs, sampling and question wording needed
claims.recommended_phrasing: The infrastructure layer has a politics: an Axios/Milltown poll (June 2026) found about half of respondents back a temporary ban on new data-center builds, with the backlash reading as a proxy for broader AI anxiety — public resistance is now a real counterforce that can slow deployment, independent of grid physics.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: У инфраструктурного слоя есть политика: опрос Axios/Milltown (июнь 2026) показал, что около половины респондентов поддерживают временный запрет на новые стройки дата-центров, а сам backlash читается как прокси более широкой тревоги об AI — общественное сопротивление теперь реальная противодействующая сила, способная замедлять развёртывание независимо от физики сети.

claims.claim: War is becoming a data flywheel for military AI: in Ukraine, where Reuters reports the military is currently the largest AI consumer and which plans domestic AI compute, a corpus of 500,000+ hours of conflict drone footage is reported to be used to train computer-vision and autonomous-drone models.

sources.title: 500k+ hours of Ukraine drone footage to train AI models (TechRadar)
Ukraine domestic AI compute; military largest AI consumer (Reuters)

caveats: Drone-footage corpus is tech-press/company-reported; needs procurement, partner and dataset-governance trail
Frame as a data flywheel, NOT as proof of fielded fully autonomous targeting
corrections: Do not claim fully autonomous targeting from this evidence alone
claims.recommended_phrasing: War is a data flywheel: operational use generates the training data that feeds the next models. In Ukraine the military is reportedly the largest AI consumer and is moving toward sovereign compute, with 500,000+ hours of drone footage reported as training data for vision and autonomous-drone systems. Phrase this as a telemetry/learning-curve advantage, not as evidence that fully autonomous targeting is fielded.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Война — это data flywheel: боевое применение порождает обучающие данные, питающие следующие модели. В Украине военные, по сообщениям, крупнейший потребитель AI и движутся к суверенному compute, при 500 000+ часах дрон-видео как обучающих данных для систем зрения и автономных дронов. Формулируйте как преимущество телеметрии/кривой обучения, а не как доказательство развёрнутого полностью автономного таргетинга.

claims.topic: Mass militarization of the AI/drone layer (South Korea)
claims.claim: Military AI is entering mass force-structure planning: South Korea announced plans to train 500,000 drone operators and deploy tens of thousands of drones (incl. AI-based swarm systems) while avoiding Chinese components, though drone targets were scaled down (from 110,000 by 2029 to ~60,000, ~11,000 expected in 2026); a preregistered nine-country survey (n=9,000) finds publics conditionally permissive toward military AI, with unease concentrated on fully autonomous lethal force.

sources.title: South Korea to train 500,000 drone operators (Reuters)
Preregistered nine-country survey on military AI (arXiv 2605.25196)

caveats: Drone/operator figures are procurement plans, scaled down from earlier goals
Component-sovereignty (no-Chinese-parts) is a production-structure signal
Public legitimacy is scenario-dependent; opposition to autonomy can still constrain deployment
claims.recommended_phrasing: Military AI is moving from elite labs into mass force structure: South Korea plans to train 500,000 drone operators and field tens of thousands of drones with AI swarms while excluding Chinese components — a security-and-production-structure move — even as targets were trimmed. A nine-country survey (n=9,000) suggests publics are conditionally permissive, with the red line at fully autonomous lethal force, so legitimacy enables scale-up but constrains full autonomy.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Военный AI выходит из элитных лабораторий в массовую структуру сил: Южная Корея планирует подготовить 500 000 операторов дронов и развернуть десятки тысяч дронов с AI-роями, исключая китайские компоненты — ход в структуре безопасности и производства — хотя цели урезаны. Девятистрановый опрос (n=9 000) показывает: публика условно допускает военный AI, с красной линией на полностью автономной летальной силе, так что легитимность разрешает масштабирование, но ограничивает полную автономию.

claims.topic: Finance moves from AI adoption to governed shutdown (kill switch)
claims.claim: Financial AI is shifting from adoption to formal operational governance: the RBI's draft AI/ML model-risk framework (June 2026, feedback to 24 July 2026) adds board accountability, independent validation, human oversight and corrective-action/decommissioning ('kill-switch') logic, alongside US bank-regulator scrutiny of AI in lending/KYC/sanctions and UK finance AI stress tests.

sources.title: RBI proposes guidelines for banks to manage AI risks (Reuters/Economic Times)

caveats: Draft rule; final obligations, supervisory enforcement and actual kill-switch maturity remain unknown
'Kill switch' framing is from secondary coverage (Economic Times); the draft uses decommissioning/corrective-action language
claims.recommended_phrasing: Critical-business AI is being governed, not just adopted: the RBI's June 2026 draft requires board accountability, independent validation, human oversight and the ability to take corrective action up to decommissioning a model — coverage frames this as a 'kill switch' — with parallel US bank-regulator scrutiny and UK finance stress tests. This is strong evidence for the critical-decision-support governance layer, though final rules and implementation maturity are still open.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Критически-бизнесовый AI теперь управляют, а не просто внедряют: июньский 2026 draft RBI требует board accountability, независимую валидацию, human oversight и возможность корректирующих действий вплоть до decommissioning модели — в прессе это называют 'kill switch' — при параллельном надзоре банковских регуляторов США и стресс-тестах в финансах UK. Это сильное свидетельство слоя governance критического decision-support, хотя финальные правила и зрелость внедрения ещё открыты.

claims.topic: Decision sovereignty: supplier boundary control and its mitigation
claims.claim: The precise strategic risk once private models enter military workflows is not 'a private model commands the army' but erosion of decision sovereignty — the supplier can influence versions, filters, usage boundaries and availability; a 2026 framework argues this is mitigable by keeping routing, constraints, logging, escalation and action-authorization state-owned and treating vendor models as replaceable modules.

sources.title: Decision-sovereignty framework for military AI (arXiv 2604.20867)
Palantir Maven becomes program-of-record (expanded)

caveats: Framework is a preprint; needs procurement examples actually implementing replaceability
Mitigation is conditional on the state genuinely owning orchestration
corrections: Frame the risk as supplier boundary control, not 'AI commands the military'
claims.recommended_phrasing: State the risk precisely: embedding vendor models in military workflows erodes decision sovereignty — the supplier shapes versions, filters, usage limits and availability — but it is not fatal. The governance remedy is sovereign orchestration: keep routing, constraints, logging, escalation and action-authorization state-owned and treat vendor models as replaceable modules. Use this as the solution section, paired with the Palantir/Maven dependence evidence.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Сформулируйте риск точно: встраивание вендорских моделей в военные процессы размывает decision sovereignty — поставщик формирует версии, фильтры, границы применения и доступность — но это не фатально. Управленческое решение — суверенная оркестрация: держать routing, ограничения, логирование, эскалацию и авторизацию действий в собственности государства, а вендорские модели — как заменяемые модули. Используйте как раздел-решение в паре с доказательствами зависимости Palantir/Maven.

claims.claim: DOJ prosecutions quantify material chip-control leakage AND escalating enforcement: the SDNY Super Micro case (unsealed Mar 2026, the largest US AI-hardware export prosecution) alleges ~$2.5B of Nvidia servers routed to China via a SE Asian shell (~$510M moved in weeks); ALX Solutions (Aug 2025) ran 20+ shipments through Singapore/Malaysia; Janford Realtor (Nov 2025) exported 400 A100 via Malaysia/Thailand with $3.89M in PRC wires; and Taiwan opened its first criminal chip-smuggling prosecution (May 2026) as Malaysia began requiring AI-chip permits.

sources.title: Two Chinese Nationals charged (ALX Solutions, transshipment via Singapore/Malaysia) — DOJ
U.S. Citizens and Chinese Nationals Arrested (Janford, 400 A100 via Malaysia/Thailand) — DOJ
Super Micro $2.5B smuggling indictment; Taiwan first prosecution (TechTimes/Reuters/Tom's)

caveats: Indictments are allegations (Super Micro/Janford not yet proven; Liaw pled not guilty, one defendant a fugitive)
These are documented case values, NOT a total gray-market leakage estimate — total volume remains unquantified (GAP residual)
Transit hubs (Taiwan/Singapore/Malaysia) historically under-policed; enforcement is now escalating
corrections: Cite case-level values, not a total leakage figure; pair leakage with the enforcement escalation
claims.recommended_phrasing: Transshipment is real and large — the SDNY Super Micro case alleges ~$2.5B routed to China (about $510M in weeks), with separate DOJ cases (ALX via Singapore/Malaysia; Janford's 400 A100 via Malaysia/Thailand) — but enforcement is escalating in parallel (DOJ's largest-ever AI-hardware prosecution, Taiwan's first criminal case, Malaysia's new permits and a June seizure). The honest reading: controls leak yet impose growing cost, delay, risk and policing — leverage need not be airtight to be structural, and total leakage volume is still unquantified.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Transshipment реален и масштабен — дело Super Micro (SDNY) вменяет ~$2.5 млрд, направленных в Китай (около $510 млн за недели), плюс отдельные дела DOJ (ALX через Сингапур/Малайзию; Janford — 400 A100 через Малайзию/Таиланд) — но enforcement параллельно растёт (крупнейшее в истории США дело по AI-железу, первое уголовное дело Тайваня, новые разрешения Малайзии и июньское изъятие). Честный вывод: контроль протекает, но накладывает растущие цену, задержку, риск и полицирование — leverage не обязан быть герметичным, чтобы быть структурным, а суммарный объём утечки всё ещё не оценён.

claims.topic: Energy/grid constraint is materially binding the buildout
claims.claim: The energy bottleneck is materially constraining the US AI buildout, not just rhetorical: Sightline Climate/Bloomberg find only ~5 GW of ~16 GW of announced 2026 US data-center capacity is under construction (30-50% delayed or cancelled), with transformer lead times of 3-5 years, switchgear sold out through 2028, interconnection queues of 3-7 years, plus tariffs and community opposition (Data Center Watch: 75+ projects / ~$130B blocked in Q1 2026; cancellations rose 2 to 6 to 25 across 2023-2025).

sources.title: Up to half of the world's data centers may be delayed this year (Latitude Media / Sightline)
75+ data-center build-outs worth $130B blocked in early 2026 (Tom's Hardware / Data Center Watch)

caveats: Aggregator figures (Sightline, Data Center Watch, Heatmap, Baird) vary by definition and methodology
Capital and demand stay high ($650B hyperscaler capex; demand 80 GW 2025 -> 150 GW 2028) — the gap is physical/political
A standardized project-level registry is still missing
claims.recommended_phrasing: Energy is not just a layer in theory but a binding constraint in practice: only ~5 of ~16 GW of announced 2026 US data-center capacity is actually being built, with 30-50% delayed or cancelled on transformers (3-5yr), switchgear (through 2028), interconnection (3-7yr) and a bipartisan local backlash (75+ projects / ~$130B blocked in Q1 2026). This anchors the bottom of the stack — and, paired with the flexibility evidence, shows the constraint is real but regionally and technologically contingent.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Энергия — не просто слой в теории, а связывающее ограничение на практике: из ~16 ГВт анонсированной на 2026 мощности дата-центров в США строится лишь ~5 ГВт, 30-50% отложено/отменено из-за трансформаторов (3-5 лет), switchgear (до 2028), подключения к сети (3-7 лет) и двухпартийного локального backlash (75+ проектов / ~$130 млрд заблокировано за Q1 2026). Это закрепляет нижний слой стека — и в паре с доказательствами гибкости показывает, что ограничение реально, но регионально и технологически обусловлено.

claims.claim: 2022-2023 were the formation phase of the AI-stack structural-power regime: industrial policy and first chip controls in 2022; model deployment, cloud lock-in, soft-law governance, China counter-controls, outbound investment controls and export-control loophole closure in 2023.

sources.title: CHIPS and Science Act, Congress.gov
Introducing ChatGPT, OpenAI
NIST AI RMF 1.0
EO 14110, Federal Register

caveats: Backfill adds foundation events; it does not replace 2025-2026 as the densest evidence period.
corrections: Do not render timeline with only one 2022 event and zero 2023 events.
claims.recommended_phrasing: The pre-2024 period is the formation phase: 2022 creates the industrial-policy and export-control base; 2023 turns the stack into a public, cloud-financed, governed and contested infrastructure.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Период до 2024 — это не пустота, а фаза сборки режима: 2022 даёт промышленную политику и первый экспортный контроль; 2023 превращает стек в публичную, cloud-financed, регулируемую и оспариваемую инфраструктуру.

claims.claim: The statement→workaround→closure pattern is visible already in 2022-2023: October 2022 controls led to Nvidia's China-specific A800/H800 adaptations, and the October 2023 BIS update tightened controls to close those loopholes.

sources.title: AP: Commerce updates policies to stop China getting advanced chips
Reuters: Nvidia offers new advanced chip for China that meets U.S. export controls

caveats: Add primary BIS rule for thresholds; Reuters URL may need archive access.
corrections: Do not show controls as one clean 2022 event followed by silence; show the loop.
claims.recommended_phrasing: Export controls were a loop from the start: rule → compliant downgraded SKU → updated rule. The A800/H800 episode is the early prototype of the later H20/H200/Blackwell toll-and-throttle logic.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: Экспортный контроль с самого начала был циклом: правило → урезанный compliant SKU → обновление правила. Эпизод A800/H800 — ранний прототип более поздней логики H20/H200/Blackwell.

claims.claim: 2023 is the governance-shock year: NIST AI RMF, China genAI Interim Measures, G7 Hiroshima Code, U.S. EO 14110, Bletchley Declaration and EU AI Act provisional agreement all frame frontier/generative AI as a risk-management and governance object.

sources.title: NIST AI RMF
CAC Interim Measures
Bletchley Declaration
EU AI Act provisional agreement

caveats: Some instruments are voluntary or provisional; distinguish hard law from soft law.
claims.recommended_phrasing: 2023 is when frontier/generative AI became a formal governance object across regimes: U.S. voluntary RMF and EO, Chinese binding service rules, G7/UK frontier-safety diplomacy, and EU risk-based market regulation.
claims.recommended_phrasing_ru: 2023 — год, когда frontier/generative AI стал формальным объектом governance: добровольный RMF и EO в США, обязательные сервисные правила в Китае, G7/UK diplomacy по frontier safety и EU risk-based market regulation.

claim_checks.claim: AI competition is now an infrastructure and capital race, not only a model/software race.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Гонка frontier-AI всё больше проходит через чипы, энергетику, дата-центры, cloud capacity и долгий capex, а не только через релизы моделей.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: If capex plans are cancelled or capacity is not deployed, use as 'announced buildout' rather than realized infrastructure.

claim_checks.claim: AI-stack access is becoming a world system of tiers.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Политика США уже двигалась к явному тирингу доступа к AI-чипам и compute, но конкретная архитектура режима нестабильна и требует датированной формулировки.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: A broad successor rule that abandons tiering, or large-scale leakage through third countries.

claim_checks.claim: Gulf sovereign AI is better described as conditional access than full-stack sovereignty.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Акторы Залива покупают доступ к frontier compute и deployment capacity, часто в связке с внешними assurance/alignment условиями; это ближе к управляемому доступу, чем к full-stack sovereignty.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Proof of independently controlled chips, domestic foundation-model training stack, cloud, security and governance without external assurance.

claim_checks.claim: Export controls convert private AI chip sales into geopolitical leverage.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Государственный экспортный контроль уже материально меняет частные продажи AI-чипов, выручку и deployment-решения, превращая коммерческую инфраструктуру в геополитический рычаг.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: If licensing is broadly waived or enforcement collapses.

claim_checks.claim: Europe is sovereign in regulation and public compute ambitions, but constrained in cloud/frontier capacity.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: ЕС усиливает публичный compute и industrial policy, но зависимость от hyperscaler cloud и внешних frontier-инфраструктур остаётся одним из главных ограничений.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Operating EU gigafactories and EU-controlled cloud capacity at frontier scale.

claim_checks.claim: Machine-speed vulnerability discovery/fix is advancing; autonomous offense at strategic scale is not yet established.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: AIxCC и OSS-CRS показывают реальный прогресс в автоматизированном finding-and-fixing; публично надёжное доказательство autonomous offensive exploit-at-scale пока спорно.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Independently verified incidents of AI-orchestrated exploit chains at scale.

claim_checks.claim: AI-mediated decision support creates a new hidden influence surface.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Prompt/RAG/agent injection уже наблюдается в реальных входах и workflow; это делает AI-mediated decision support правдоподобным каналом скрытого влияния, хотя государственное применение такого канала пока требует отдельных кейсов.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Robust agent architectures that reliably separate data from instructions, plus lack of production incidents.

claim_checks.claim: In 2026, frontier cyber-capable models themselves became export-control objects.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: В июне 2026 экспортно-контрольная логика перешла с чипов и дата-центров на уровень самих frontier/cyber-моделей: Anthropic временно отключила Mythos/Fable после сообщения о правительственном order, а OpenAI ограничила GPT-5.6-preview по запросу правительства. До публикации первичных order/license документов это надо формулировать как reported/confirmed-by-company access control, а не как полностью реконструированное право.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Publication of narrow voluntary-review terms showing no binding export-control effect, or full restoration without continuing restrictions.

claim_checks.claim: Mythos proves machine-speed 0-day discovery has arrived.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Mythos-related reporting and Mozilla-style defender evidence strongly suggest a step-change in AI-assisted vulnerability discovery; however, independent rediscovery tests and classified-test caveats mean we should not state that autonomous machine-speed 0-day exploitation is established.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Open benchmark with repeated autonomous discovery, validation and patch/exploit-chain reproduction across diverse real targets.

claim_checks.claim: 360 has built a Chinese Mythos equivalent.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: 360 заявляет о китайской версии Mythos — Tulongfeng — и об автоматизированной защитной системе Yitianzhen; Reuters подтверждает сам факт заявления и числа как утверждения компании, но прямо отмечает, что не смогла независимо проверить capability claims.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Independent benchmark showing much lower results, inability to verify the 105 regulator-confirmed vulnerabilities, or evidence that findings are low-severity/noise.

claim_checks.claim: China can route around weaker base models through agent harness and security data.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Zhou/360 формулируют китайский ответ не как прямую гонку за самой сильной базовой моделью, а как agent-harness путь: модель + security data + инструменты + память + многоагентная координация. Это важный стратегический нарратив и plausible engineering hypothesis, но пока не доказанный parity-result.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Independent tasks showing harness fails without frontier base models/chips.

claim_checks.claim: AI is already used in critical state and business decision-support domains.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: ИИ уже встроен в критические workflow: военное C2/mission planning/targeting-support, банковские lending/KYC/sanctions-screening контуры, инвестиционно-банковскую подготовку материалов и киберзащиту legacy-инфраструктуры. Но по военным кейсам нужно говорить 'decision support / targeting support', а не 'автономное убийство'.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Primary documents showing deployments are pilots with no operational impact; lack of active users or production integration.

claim_checks.claim: Control over AI capabilities often appears as quiet access control rather than public bans.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Помимо публичных банов и экспортных правил, контроль реализуется тихо: review/approval для national-security uses, human-review requirements для high-stakes automation, tiered access, enhanced due diligence, vetted exemptions для dual-use science, trusted organizations и monitoring classifiers.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: If providers publish evidence that these controls are rarely used or merely symbolic.

claim_checks.claim: Biotech/CBRN capabilities are already governed by special model-level access controls.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: По CBRN/biotech виден особый режим: провайдеры строят real-time/async фильтры и мониторинг, а легитимным dual-use пользователям могут давать vetted exemptions вместо полного отказа. Это и есть слой 'governance by access'.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Evidence that bio-related restrictions are generic refusals only and do not affect access decisions or exemptions.

claim_checks.claim: Once private AI models enter military workflows, suppliers can influence operational boundary conditions.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Встраивание коммерческих моделей в военные workflow создаёт новую проблему decision sovereignty: государство хочет 'any lawful use', провайдеры хотят guardrails, а итоговая граница применения задаётся контрактами, review procedures и техническими фильтрами.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Clear architecture where state-owned orchestration fully isolates final action policy from supplier models and provider vetoes.

claim_checks.claim: Palantir is best understood as an operating system for institutional decision power, not just a software vendor.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Palantir лучше читать как слой операционной власти: он собирает данные, строит operational ontology, подмешивает AI/LLM, задаёт интерфейс решений, права доступа, audit trail и workflow для военных, полиции, здравоохранения, миграционного контроля и бизнеса.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: If primary documents show limited dashboard-only deployments with no decision workflow or operational dependency.

claim_checks.claim: Palantir creates land-and-expand / vendor-lock-in dynamics inside public-sector decision infrastructure.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: В нескольких кейсах Palantir входит через ограниченный/пилотный или срочный контракт, а затем расширяет scope и стоимость; но для строгого утверждения о lock-in нужно показывать procurement records и switching-cost rationale по каждому кейсу.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Competitive re-procurement with low switching costs and comparable alternatives.

claim_checks.claim: Palantir is becoming a U.S.-anchored alliance decision platform.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Maven/MSS и связанные контракты делают Palantir одним из главных U.S.-anchored software layers для AI-enabled warfighting и allied C2/interoperability. Это усиливает тезис о platform-affiliate sovereignty внутри НАТО.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: If European/NATO domestic alternatives replace Palantir without operational loss.

claim_checks.claim: Palantir controls the data of its public-sector clients.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Не надо писать, что Palantir 'владеет' или 'контролирует' NHS/state data. Безопаснее: Palantir supplies the operational software layer through which public institutions integrate, query and act on sensitive data; formal ownership/control may remain with the state, while dependency and access-control risks remain contested.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Primary contracts showing no external access, strong state-side key/control, open portability and independent audit.

claim_checks.claim: The AI capital race is extreme but unequal, and private figures understate China.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: По Stanford AI Index 2026: глобальные частные инвестиции $344.7 млрд (+127.5%), корпоративные $581.7 млрд (+130%); США $285.9 млрд против $12.4 млрд у Китая (23.1x). Частные цифры занижают Китай: guidance-фонды дали ~$184 млрд в AI (2000-2023); цифра $912 млрд — это все отрасли, не AI.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Revised AI Index methodology or a recount that narrows the gap; do not cite $912B as AI-specific.

claim_checks.claim: The chip export regime is a graduated toll-and-throttle system, not a simple ban.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: К 2026 чиповый режим — дозированный 'вентиль с пошлиной': H200/MI325X на case-by-case (25% пошлина, 50% потолок, KYC, инспекция США), Blackwell под denial, H20 снова идёт по схеме с 15%; Пекин дросселирует со своей стороны, Huawei целится в 600 тыс. Ascend 910C. Формулируйте как метеринг доступа по поколению/цене/слежке, а не как эмбарго.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: A broad successor rule that abandons case-by-case metering, or Beijing fully opening to imports.

claim_checks.claim: Export controls are leaky but still structural.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Контроль не герметичен: есть smuggling/transshipment через третьи страны. Но именно поэтому третьи страны превращаются в узлы контроля, а стек — в объект таможни, лицензирования, KYC и инспекций. Правильная формула: leverage does not need to be airtight to be structural.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Evidence that route-around volume dwarfs licensed/controlled supply and enforcement has no material effect on cost, delay or risk.

claim_checks.claim: Energy/grid capacity is a hard AI-stack layer, but flexibility can partially mitigate it.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Энергия — нижний слой AI-стека: рост load создаёт региональные bottlenecks и политическую сопротивляемость. Но дата-центры не обязательно являются полностью негибкими нагрузками: workload orchestration и load shifting могут снижать часть grid stress. Это не отменяет энергетическую власть, а делает её управляемой и локально разной.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Large-scale proof that flexible compute eliminates interconnection delays and capacity expansion needs across major regions.

claim_checks.claim: War creates a data flywheel for military AI.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Украина показывает data-telemetry слой войны: военное применение создаёт массивы видео/сенсорных данных, которые затем обучают модели для распознавания, навигации и автономных систем. Пока это лучше формулировать как data flywheel, а не как доказательство fully autonomous targeting.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Evidence that drone-footage datasets are not used operationally or do not improve models beyond synthetic baselines.

claim_checks.claim: Finance is moving from AI adoption to AI operational-governance controls.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: В финансах AI уже не просто productivity tool: регуляторы требуют model inventory, independent validation, board accountability, human oversight, corrective actions и возможность отключения/decommissioning. Это сильный бизнес-критический слой decision-support governance.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Final rules remove decommissioning/kill-switch logic, or banks show these models are only low-risk pilots.

claim_checks.claim: Private model suppliers can affect military decision boundaries, but architecture can reduce dependency.
claim_checks.safe_wording_ru: Самый точный риск — не 'частная модель командует армией', а erosion of decision sovereignty: поставщик может влиять на версии, фильтры, границы использования и доступность. Но это не фатально: model replaceability, state-owned routing/logging/escalation/action authorization могут снижать зависимость.
claim_checks.what_could_break_it: Procurement architectures showing full state-owned orchestration and easy model replacement across major military AI systems.

rented_or_external_layers: some manufacturing dependence on TSMC/ASML global supply chain

controlled_layers: some AI accelerators and data center buildout
strongest_counter_evidence: U.S. advanced chip controls and D:5 guidance
Conditional access is still access; UAE may accumulate operational capacity over time.

country_cards.tier_position: Gulf sovereign AI buyer / finance-rent power
key_dependencies: xAI and other foreign frontier providers
strongest_counter_evidence: Investment stake does not equal stack control.

country_cards.tier_position: semi-periphery with regulation, public compute and strategic chips/supply-chain assets
managed dependence / sovereignty gap with growing market and cloud buildout
strongest_counter_evidence: Domestic compute mission may improve exit option if deployed at scale.

country_cards.tier_position: isolated autarky / cut-out stack with forced localization
rented_or_external_layers: some open-weight or China-friendly model paths
strongest_counter_evidence: Actual compute baseline remains under-measured; Nebius should not be counted as Russian sovereign stack after split.

contested_or_needs_more_independent_vali.description: Anthropic/Qihoo/vendor claims about AI-assisted or AI-orchestrated cyber operations should be coded as disputed unless accompanied by independent IoCs, technical reports or third-party validation.

cyber_pack.safe_summary_ru: Сильная доказательная база есть для automated find-and-fix в соревнованиях и preprint follow-up; утверждение об autonomous offensive exploitation at strategic scale требует отдельного доказательства.

threat_map.threat: Indirect prompt injection in web/RAG inputs
threat_map.mechanism: Hidden machine-facing instructions embedded in webpages or HTTP responses steer model output or agent actions.
threat_map.gap: Need production incidents in government/enterprise RAG.

threat_map.threat: Prompt injection in hiring / resume screening
threat_map.mechanism: Applicants embed hidden instructions in resumes consumed by LLM-based screening workflows.
threat_map.gap: Need more platforms and non-hiring workflows.

threat_map.threat: Unsafe agentic deployment with weak transparency
threat_map.mechanism: Deployed agents act across tools while public safety/evaluation transparency remains uneven.
threat_map.gap: Need deployment registry for government and critical infrastructure agents.

cognitive_security.safe_summary_ru: Риск когнитивного влияния лучше формулировать не как доказанную госоперацию, а как уже измеримую поверхность атаки в AI-mediated workflows.

critical_decision_use.safe_summary_ru: Критический слой теперь двоякий: AI уже входит в военные/финансовые decision chains, а доступ к наиболее чувствительным возможностям регулируется не только законом, но и тихими платформенными механизмами — review, vetting, monitoring, trusted users and exemptions.

palantir_case.position_in_thesis: Palantir is a practical example of structural-power compression at the decision-support layer: security + knowledge + production + finance.
core_mechanism: operational ontology and common operating picture
permissioning, audit trails and human-in-the-loop interfaces
strongest_supporting_facts: NHS FDP £330M contract and access controversy
strongest_counterarguments: Palantir often acts as processor/vendor rather than legal data owner
critical performance claims may lack causal proof
many contracts are ceilings or frameworks, not spent money
some military targeting claims rely on classified or anonymous-source reporting
public-sector resistance and procurement scrutiny can interrupt expansion
palantir_case.recommended_article_frame_ru: Palantir — не 'модель' и не 'облако', а слой операционного мышления: он превращает разрозненные данные государства и корпораций в actionable workflows. Поэтому он особенно хорошо показывает knowledge-structure capture: власть возникает не только из владения compute, а из контроля над тем, как институт видит ситуацию, ранжирует риск и выбирает действие.

Guidance-fund clarification: $184B AI vs $912B all-industry. Residual: pull exact PDF table cells for footnotes.

gaps.topic: Pledge vs committed vs allocated vs spent vs deployed

gaps.why: Core counterargument to stack-dependence thesis.
gaps.what_would_close: Compare DeepSeek/Qwen/Kimi/Huawei Ascend/SMIC stack with cloud, chips, training curves, inference cost and deployment capacity.

Residual: government still has not published the official order text; legal validity contested and in litigation.

gaps.why: Regulators ask for controls, but deployment maturity is unclear.

Residual: final circulars, supervisory findings, and proof of actual decommissioning implementations (see GAP_023).

gaps.why: Guardian/+972 evidence is important but contested and based on anonymous sources.

gaps.why: Several key claims rely on press reports and contract ceilings.
gaps.what_would_close: DoD, Army, NATO, UK MoD, NHS, ICE contract documents with scope, data-rights, access, portability and audit terms.
gaps.resolution: Primary record captured (SIG_2025_PALANTIR_ARMY_10B_PRIMARY): army.mil release + federal award W519TC-25-D-0039, 10-yr IDC, $10B ceiling, $0 obligated at award, 75 contracts consolidated. Money-discipline correction logged (ceiling != spend).

gaps.what_would_close: Independent audits of Maven targeting accuracy, NHS FDP outcomes, ICE enforcement outcomes, Met Police false positives.

gaps.topic: Scale of AI-chip smuggling and transshipment via Malaysia/Singapore/UAE

Case values: ~$2.5B scheme ($510M in weeks), 400 A100, 20+ SG/MY shipments. Residual: total gray-market leakage volume remains unquantified.

gaps.topic: Hard project-level data on delayed/cancelled AI data-center projects
gaps.why: Backlash and grid stress are critical counterforces, but project-delay totals are often advocacy/aggregator figures.
gaps.what_would_close: Project-level list with status, capacity, value, developer, jurisdiction, reason for delay and primary local records.
gaps.resolution: Hard data captured (SIG_2026_DATACENTER_CANCELLATIONS_HARD_DATA): Sightline/Bloomberg ~5 of 16 GW under construction (30-50% delayed/cancelled); Data Center Watch 75+ projects / $130B blocked Q1; Heatmap 20 cancelled / $41.7B / 3.5GW Q1; Baird 2->6->25 cancellations. Residual: standardized project-level registry with primary local records.

gaps.topic: Primary evidence for Ukrainian/Russian autonomous targeting and drone-model data pipelines
gaps.why: War data flywheel is central but much current evidence is company/media reporting.
gaps.what_would_close: Government procurement, company release, model card, testing results, or fielded-system documentation without operational attack details.

gaps.why: Draft rules show direction, but final obligations and enforcement remain open.
gaps.what_would_close: Final circulars, supervisory examination findings, bank model inventories and evidence of kill-switch/decommissioning implementations.

gaps.topic: Public legitimacy thresholds for military AI by country and scenario
gaps.why: Military AI adoption may be more politically feasible than expected, but autonomy red lines differ.
gaps.what_would_close: Peer-reviewed cross-national survey, longitudinal polling and country-specific elite/policy responses.

counterarguments.objection: The tier taxonomy already exists (New America, TBI, RAND, Forrester).
counterarguments.steelman: Multiple think tanks have published AI-stack tier maps; a typology is not a novel contribution.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Concede the typology; locate the contribution in the MECHANISM — how export controls convert private sales into rent/leverage, and how the knowledge structure is captured at the same layer as security.
counterarguments.evidence_status: Accepted — reframe contribution, do not claim novelty of the map.

counterarguments.objection: AI colonialism / technofeudalism framings are contested.
counterarguments.steelman: These are polemical and empirically loose; relying on them invites dismissal.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Make Strange structural power the load-bearing frame; cite colonialism/feudalism only as rhetorical color, not analysis.
counterarguments.evidence_status: Accepted — use structural power as primary lens.

counterarguments.steelman: The GTG-1002 case lacks IoCs and corroboration; AIxCC is synthetic and time-boxed.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Use machine-speed cyber as a stress-test scenario; rely on AIxCC/Big Sleep/OSS-CRS as the verified floor, flag GTG-1002 as disputed.

counterarguments.objection: Defense can scale too (AIxCC, open-source CRS), weakening offense-dominance.
counterarguments.steelman: The same tools that find bugs were open-sourced for defenders; net balance is uncertain.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Drop offense-dominance; argue from ACCESS ASYMMETRY (who has the compute/tools) rather than inherent attacker advantage.

counterarguments.objection: Compute controls leak (smuggling, efficiency, smaller models, agent route).
counterarguments.steelman: DeepSeek efficiency, gray-market routing via Singapore/Malaysia/UAE, and distillation erode control.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Concede leakage; argue controls still impose cost, delay and surveillance (BIS D:5 guidance, Chip Security Act) — leverage need not be airtight to be structural.
counterarguments.evidence_status: Partially accepted — leakage is real but does not negate leverage. Now anchored by SIG_2026_MALAYSIA_AI_CHIP_SMUGGLING_SEIZURE (concrete seizure) and ca-08. Strongly anchored by DOJ cases (export-18): Super Micro ~$2.5B, ALX via Singapore/Malaysia, Janford 400 A100 — leakage is real, but so is escalating enforcement (Taiwan/Malaysia).

counterarguments.objection: Open weights may not equal sovereignty (learning curves, tacit iteration).
counterarguments.steelman: Open weights change the metropole but do not transfer fabs, HBM, tacit know-how or tempo.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Adopt as a central finding (openweight-02); distinguish exit at the model layer from dependence at the compute layer.

counterarguments.objection: Private in form is not always state in function — need concrete control mechanisms, not slogans.
counterarguments.steelman: Calling Nvidia a state instrument is sloppy; it is a private firm.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Point to SPECIFIC mechanisms: BIS licensing, the 15% revenue-share, GP10/D:5 extraterritoriality, G42 forced divestiture, DoD contracts, Chip Security Act tracking — control via regulation, not ownership.
counterarguments.evidence_status: Accepted — use named mechanisms, not state-company rhetoric.

counterarguments.objection: Export controls are porous; smuggling and third-country routing undercut the chokepoint thesis.
counterarguments.steelman: If advanced chips can be moved through Malaysia/Singapore/UAE or accessed through foreign cloud, controls may raise friction but not determine capability.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Concede leakage; argue structural power is cost/delay/risk/surveillance, not perfect denial. Add Malaysian seizure as both evidence of leakage and of enforcement/policing.

counterarguments.objection: Energy constraints are overplayed because AI compute can become flexible load.
counterarguments.steelman: Recent work shows GPU clusters can curtail, shift and migrate workloads, potentially reducing grid costs and interconnection bottlenecks.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Adopt a nuanced version: energy is a structural layer, but its constraint is region-specific and can be managed by software-defined compute flexibility.

counterarguments.objection: Private suppliers do not necessarily erode state sovereignty if the state owns orchestration.
counterarguments.steelman: A military system can preserve decision sovereignty by keeping routing, constraints, logging and action authorization state-owned and treating vendor models as replaceable modules.
counterarguments.how_to_handle: Use this as the governance solution section: the risk is supplier boundary control; the remedy is model replaceability and sovereign orchestration.

top_5_news_that_changed_thesis: Palantir makes the thesis more concrete: the relevant control layer is not only compute/model access, but operational decision infrastructure.
Maven becoming program-of-record plus NATO MSS acquisition turns Palantir into an allied C2/platform-affiliation case.
NHS FDP and ICE ImmigrationOS show the same stack logic in civilian state functions: health and migration enforcement.
UK MoD and Met Police cases show procurement and vendor-lock-in are part of structural power, not merely administrative noise.
Palantir counterarguments are strong and useful: data ownership may stay with the state, performance claims may lack causal proof, and many amounts are ceilings/frameworks.
Critical military use: Maven becoming a Pentagon program of record makes AI command-support a durable state capability, not a one-off pilot.
Classified model access: Google/OpenAI/AWS/others entering classified networks shows frontier models are moving into sensitive mission workflows.
Ukraine compute: Ukraine's military being the largest AI consumer links battlefield AI directly to domestic compute sovereignty.
Finance: U.S. and Indian regulators are probing/mandating AI governance around lending, KYC, sanctions screening, model inventories, kill switches and human oversight.
Quiet control: OpenAI policy review/approval and Anthropic ASL-3 due diligence/vetted exemptions show governance happens through access layers, not only through public bans.
Model-layer controls: Anthropic Mythos/Fable and OpenAI GPT-5.6 show the control object is moving from chips to model access itself.
Trusted-access regime: Mythos 5 restoration to vetted U.S. critical-infrastructure organizations operationalizes model-tier access.
China response: 360's Yitian Tulong/Tulongfeng/Yitianzhen narrative frames vulnerability-finding AI as strategic deterrence and one-way-transparency countermeasure.
Counter-evidence: Mythos-linked rediscovery benchmark tempers zero-day-factory claims and supports reviewer-proofing.
Cognitive/cyber overlap: Fable 5 red-team results support pre-release government concern but should not be conflated with real-world cyber exploitation.
BIS May 31, 2026 D:5 guidance shows ownership/headquarters-based advanced-compute controls survive AI Diffusion Rule turbulence.
EU preliminary AWS/Azure gatekeeper finding shows cloud concentration is becoming an autonomy and competition issue.
Amazon's $13B India AI/cloud announcement shows major Global South AI capacity growth still routed through U.S. hyperscalers.
Russia's proposed foreign-AI restriction powers strengthen the isolated-autarky country card.
AIxCC/OSS-CRS plus prompt-injection-in-the-wild research strengthen the cyber/cognitive-security mechanism while preserving important caveats.
claims_to_upgrade: Knowledge-structure capture can be demonstrated through operational platforms, not only AI models.
Decision sovereignty is the most precise language for Palantir-style risk.
Platform-affiliate sovereignty inside NATO/UK can be anchored in Palantir/Maven evidence.
AI-stack structural power includes decision-support/cognition as a live operational layer.
Quiet access control is a load-bearing mechanism for critical and dual-use AI capabilities.
Finance is a strong second domain after military/cyber for evidence of AI in critical decision chains.
AI stack is an infrastructure/power/cloud/chip race.
Export controls convert private stack sales into geopolitical leverage.
Prompt/RAG/agent injection is an observed workflow risk, not only a lab scenario.
claims_to_downgrade: Palantir AI autonomously makes lethal decisions.
Every contract ceiling equals money spent.
Israel AI-targeting claims should remain contested/investigative, not settled legal facts.
Military AI use should be phrased as decision support / targeting support unless autonomous action is independently proven.
Provider policies should not be equated with public law; they are private governance with opaque enforcement.
Machine-speed autonomous offensive exploitation is established fact.
Gulf sovereign AI equals full-stack sovereignty.
AI Diffusion Rule created a stable world order of tiers.

top_5_news_that_changed_thesis: Malaysia's $12.93M AI-chip seizure shows controls leak, but also that transit states are being folded into the enforcement layer.
New 2026 power-system papers sharpen the energy layer: AI data centers create regional grid stress, but flexible GPU-cluster orchestration can partially mitigate it.
Ukraine's drone-data and domestic-compute stories make the data flywheel concrete: battlefield use generates training data, which drives demand for sovereign compute.
South Korea's 500,000-drone-operator plan shows military AI/drone capability entering mass force-structure planning.
RBI/finance kill-switch and validation rules show critical business AI moving from adoption to governed shutdown/decommissioning controls.
claims_to_upgrade: Control need not be airtight to be structural; enforcement plus leakage is the actual regime.
Energy is a real stack layer, but needs to be framed as local and software-manageable.
Data telemetry from war is a central source of AI advantage.
Financial AI governance is now strong evidence for critical-business decision-support controls.
claims_to_downgrade: Export controls fully block China or route-around behavior.
Energy bottlenecks are absolute and uniform.
Ukraine or drone-war evidence proves fully autonomous targeting without additional primary evidence.

timeline_backfill_2022_2023.why_added_ru: Предыдущая визуализация создавала ложную пустоту между Oct-2022 BIS controls и 2024/2025 events. Backfill показывает, что 2022-2023 — это фаза сборки режима: промышленная политика, публичный deployment shock, cloud/model lock-in, soft/hard governance, China counter-controls и первый цикл loophole closure.
visual_reading_ru: 2022: build-at-home + deny/condition abroad + ChatGPT as public deployment shock.

2023: GPT-4 and Azure/OpenAI make model/cloud dependence visible; NIST/China/G7/US/UK/EU create governance layer; China starts materials counter-controls; BIS closes A800/H800 workaround; DARPA launches AI-cyber path.

narrative_graph.purpose_ru: Явный слой сюжетных связей для визуализации по образцу ProfGames: statement→walkback, escalation, countermove, cross-layer dependency, mitigation, leakage, safe-wording correction.

node_model.existing_ref: Most nodes reference existing IDs from evidence, claims, claim_checks, counterarguments, gaps and timeline.
node_model.synthetic_ref: Synthetic thesis nodes are used only as visual anchors and do not add new factual claims.

relation_types.supports: Источник/факт прямо поддерживает claim или thesis.
relation_types.supports_with_scope: Поддерживает, но только в ограниченной формулировке.
relation_types.supports_but_limits: Поддерживает направление, но одновременно ограничивает overclaim.
relation_types.challenges_overclaim: Не опровергает факт целиком, но запрещает сильную формулировку.
relation_types.walks_back: Откат/уточнение/публичный или аналитический rollback предыдущего тезиса.
relation_types.updates: Более свежий факт меняет статус или механизм прежнего факта.
relation_types.escalates: Поднимает контроль/риск на более высокий слой стека.
relation_types.routes_around: Обход или альтернативный путь внутри стека.
relation_types.qualifies: Добавляет условие или ограничение к тезису.
relation_types.refines: Переформулирует claim в более точную версию.
relation_types.parallel: Показывает аналогичный паттерн в другом домене.
relation_types.generalizes: Переносит механизм из одного домена в другой.
relation_types.institutionalizes: Показывает переход от пилота/события к устойчивому институту.
relation_types.supports_arc: Связывает claim напрямую с сюжетной дугой.
